 54 ---

 108 - ### Session transcript capture

 109 - - Steven starts sessions with `scout` in terminal (not `claude`). This wraps Claude Code with `script` to capture full transcripts.

 110 - - Transcripts save to `docs/sessions/session_N.md`. Raw captures in `docs/sessions/.raw/` (gitignored).

 111 - - During end-of-session routine: run `python3 scripts/clean_transcript.py "$SCOUT_RAW_TRANSCRIPT" "$SCOUT_CLEAN_TRANSCRIPT"`, then include the clean file in the commit.

 112 - - Env vars available: `SCOUT_SESSION_NUM`, `SCOUT_RAW_TRANSCRIPT`, `SCOUT_CLEAN_TRANSCRIPT`.

 113 - - Steven uses `/exit` between sessions (not `/clear`) so each `scout` run = one transcript file.

 114 - - Wrapper script auto-commits as fallback if end-of-session routine is skipped.

 115 - - **Session numbering source of truth is CLAUDE.md header** (`Session N`). The `scout_session.sh` script parses this, transcript files on disk, and raw files — uses the highest. This prevents d

 - rift when sessions run without the `scout` wrapper.

 56 + ## CRITICAL RULES (top 15 — full rule set in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`)

 117 - ---

 58 + 1. **Always enter plan mode** before non-trivial builds. New scanners, strategies, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off. Session 51 shipped 7 f

 + eatures without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs. Established Session 52.

 59 + 2. **Always push code from Claude Code via git**, never `/push_code` in Telegram (4096 char truncation). `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from Claude Code terminal. Hard rule since

 + Session 19.

 60 + 3. **Read before write.** Before touching any file that calls an existing module, read that module first. Every crash in this project has been caused by hallucinated method names. Module APIs i

 + n `agent/CLAUDE.md` + `tools/CLAUDE.md`.

 61 + 4. **Every plan is one-shot; pressure-test hard BEFORE presenting.** Don't write a plan expecting refinement rounds. Steven rejected plans that pushed rigor onto him. See `memory/feedback_plans

 + _are_one_shot.md`.

 62 + 5. **Verify foundational assumptions empirically BEFORE writing plans.** Walk the pipeline end-to-end and probe the load-bearing assumptions (Serper queries, httpx fetches, grep verification).

 + BUG 1 + BUG 2 both had silent rev-1s that only surfaced via live probing.

 63 + 6. **New scanners ship with kill switches.** `ENABLE_X_SCAN = True` constant near top of `tools/signal_processor.py`. Scanner short-circuits at function entry when disabled. One-line disable in

 + production without removing code.

 64 + 7. **Multi-feature sessions → one commit per feature.** Don't bundle features into one big commit at session end. Separate commits enable surgical `git revert`.

 65 + 8. **Signal vs Prospect routing for new lead-gen scanners.** HIGH confidence → auto-queue via `district_prospector.add_district()` as `pending`. MEDIUM/LOW → Signals tab only via `write_signals

 + ()`. Active customer match → `customer_intel` log only (don't sell, don't discard). All queue writes are `pending` — Steven manually approves.

 66 + 9. **`handle_message()` and async tasks must call `get_gas_bridge()` locally** — never reference `gas` as a free variable. Python treats `gas` as local throughout the function (it's assigned la

 + ter for `/call_list`), so any earlier reference raises `Unbound Local Error`. Same for `_run_*_scan()` tasks spawned via `asyncio.create_task()`. Two latent bugs shipped in Session 49 from this p

 + attern.

 67 + 10. **Never use `requests` or `time.sleep()` inside async functions.** Use `httpx.AsyncClient` for HTTP and `await asyncio.sleep()` for delays. Synchronous versions freeze the event loop. Synch

 + ronous blocking code called from async context must use `run_in_executor`.

 68 + 11. **`global` declarations go at the TOP of `handle_message()`**, not in elif blocks. Python SyntaxError if `global` appears after first use of the variable. One line at top: `global conversat

 + ion_history, _pending_draft, _last_prospect_batch, _pending_approve_force, _csv_import_mode, _csv_import_state, _pipeline_import_mode, _pending_csv_intent, _last_proximity_result`.

 69 + 12. **`tool_result` always follows `tool_use`.** Wrap every `execute_tool()` call in try/except. A result must always be appended to conversation history, even on error. Missing `tool_result` →

 + 400 on next API call.

 70 + 13. **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call `execute_tool()` directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_impo

 + rt`, `/import_*`, `/enrich_leads`, all `/prospect_*`. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling

 + tools.

 71 + 14. **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement. **ALL sequences are drafts** — always G

 + oogle Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval. Never auto-finalize.

 72 + 15. **Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 119 - ## CRITICAL RULES

 74 + > **Full rule set** (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring tables, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops, Session 55/56/57

 + post-mortems): **`docs/SCOUT_RULES.md`** — read by section or grep by keyword.

 121 - **Always enter plan mode before building anything non-trivial.** New scanners, new strategies, new tools, schema changes, multi-file refactors — all require `Enter Plan Mode` + Steven's sign-off

 - BEFORE code gets written. Session 51 shipped 7 features without plan mode and 3 of them had BLOCKER-level bugs that Session 52 had to fix. Exception: typos, one-line config tweaks, documentatio

 - n edits. When in doubt, plan. Rule established Session 52.

 122 -

 123 - **Always push code changes from Claude Code via git — never tell Steven to use `/push_code` in Telegram.** Scout's `/push_code` dumps entire file contents into Telegram (4,096-char limit) causi

 - ng truncation and confusion. Always `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` directly from the Claude Code terminal. This is a hard rule established Session 19.

 124 -

 125 - **Read before write.** Before touching any file that calls an existing module, read that module first. Every crash in this project has been caused by hallucinated method names. Module APIs are

 - documented in `agent/CLAUDE.md` and `tools/CLAUDE.md`.

 126 -

 127 - **Always produce complete replacement files.** Never give partial snippets or "find line X and replace." Steven uploads full files via GitHub web interface.

 128 -

 129 - **Lazy imports for Phase 4+ modules.** `github_pusher`, `sequence_builder`, `fireflies`, `call_processor` are imported INSIDE `execute_tool()`, never at the top of `main.py`.

 130 -

 131 - **Top-level imports for flat tool modules.** `activity_tracker`, `csv_importer`, `daily_call_list`, `district_prospector`, `pipeline_tracker`, `lead_importer` are imported at the top of main.py

 - like sheets_writer.

 132 -

 133 - **tool_result always follows tool_use.** Wrap every `execute_tool()` call in try/except. A result must always be appended to conversation history, even on error. Missing tool_result → 400 on ne

 - xt API call.

 134 -

 135 - **Never use `requests` or `time.sleep()` inside async functions.** Use `httpx.AsyncClient` for HTTP and `await asyncio.sleep()` for delays. Both synchronous versions freeze the asyncio event lo

 - op.

 136 -

 137 - **Synchronous code called from async context must use `run_in_executor`.** Wrap blocking I/O in `await loop.run_in_executor(None, fn, args...)`. Never call blocking functions directly from asyn

 - c methods.

 138 -

 139 - **`handle_message()` and async tasks must call `get_gas_bridge()` locally — never reference `gas` as a free variable.** `handle_message()` assigns `gas = get_gas_bridge()` later in the function

 - (line ~1687 for `/call_list`). Python treats `gas` as local throughout the entire function — so any earlier reference raises `Unbound Local Error`. Same applies to `_run_*_scan()` functions spaw

 - ned via `asyncio.create_task()` from the scheduler — they don't inherit the outer `gas` local. ALWAYS call `get_gas_bridge()` into a local variable like `draft_gas` or `scan_gas` at the top of

 - any new branch or scheduled function. Two latent bugs from this pattern shipped in Session 49.

 140 -

 141 - **New scanners ship with kill switches.** Add an `ENABLE_X_SCAN = True` constant near the top of `tools/signal_processor.py` (next to `SERPER_API_KEY`). Scanner checks the flag at function entr

 - y and returns empty if disabled. One-line commit to disable in production without removing code. Examples: `ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN`, `ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN`.

 142 -

 143 - **Multi-feature sessions ship one commit per feature.** Don't bundle features into one big commit at session end. Separate commits enable surgical `git revert` if a feature causes production is

 - sues. Session 49 shipped F3 → F1 → F4 → F2 as separate commits + small fix commits.

 144 -

 145 - **Signal vs. Prospect routing for new lead-gen scanners.** HIGH confidence → auto-queue via `district_prospector.add_district()` as `pending`. MEDIUM/LOW → Signals tab only via `write_signals()

 - `. Active customer match → `customer_intel` log only (don't sell, don't discard). Pattern established in F4 + F2 scanners (Session 49). All queue writes are `pending` — Steven manually approves

 - via `/prospect_approve`. No auto-elevation logic.

 146 -

 147 - **GAS `create Draft` always passes `skip_if_draft_exists=True`.** This prevents duplicate drafts on threads that already have one. GAS-side check via `thread Has Draft(thread Id)` iterates `Gmail Ap

 - p.getDrafts()`. Returns `{success: false, already_drafted: true}` which `gas_bridge._call()` passes through (does NOT raise). Email drafter treats `already_drafted` as a soft-skip, not an error

 - .

 148 -

 149 - **`_calculate_priority()` strategies as of Session 52:** `upward` (600-999), `winback` (550-749), `proximity` (400-699), `esa_cluster` (450-599), `intra_district` (750-849), `cs_funding_recipie

 - nt` (800-899), `competitor_displacement` (650-749), `csta_partnership` (620-719), `charter_cmo` (780-899 scaled by school_count), `cte_center` (760-879 scaled by sending_districts), `private_sc

 - hool_network` (740-839 scaled by schools), `compliance_gap` (850-939 scaled by est_enrollment — applies only on manual `/signal_act` promotion since F9 is Signals-only pilot), `homeschool_coop`

 - (500-599), and falls through to `cold` (300-799 by enrollment) for anything unknown. Add new branches for new strategies — falling through to cold gives wrong sort order for warm leads. **Afte

 - r Session 52:** `add_district(**kwargs)` forwards `school_count`, `total_licenses`, `est_enrollment` positionally and the rest via kwargs — pass the size metadata or priority scoring collapses

 - to tier base.

 150 -

 151 - **`add_district(**kwargs)` and size metadata:** As of Session 52 Commit 1, `add_district()` accepts `**kwargs` and forwards them to `_calculate_priority`. Callers should pass `est_enrollment`,

 - `school_count`, `sending_districts`, or `schools` depending on strategy — these both affect priority scoring and populate queue row columns 15-17 (`Est. Enrollment`, `School Count`, `Total Lice

 - nses`). If you add a new strategy and don't pass the right kwargs, it'll score at tier base and look broken. The existing `_calculate_priority` signature has `school_count` and `total_licenses`

 - as named positionals; `add_district` pops them out of kwargs before forwarding to avoid collision. Use `functools.partial` when calling via `loop.run_in_executor(None, fn, *args)` — that API c

 - an't pass kwargs directly.

 152 -

 153 - **`/signal_act` uses `_SIGNAL_TYPE_TO_STRATEGY` dict for strategy mapping.** Added Session 52 Commit 2 in `agent/main.py`. Currently only maps `"compliance" → "compliance_gap"`. Anything else d

 - efaults to `"trigger"` which falls through to the cold branch (same as the pre-Session-52 behavior for back-compat). Session 53+ follow-up: add `bond → bond_trigger`, `leadership → leadership_c

 - hange`, `rfp → rfp_trigger`, etc. after those strategies are built. When you add a new signal-promoted strategy, you MUST also add its branch in `_calculate_priority` AND its entry in `_SIGNAL_

 - TYPE_TO_STRATEGY`.

 154 -

 155 - **F9 compliance_gap_scanner is Signals-only pilot mode.** Session 52 Commit 2 rewrote the queue path — scanner writes extracts to the Signals tab via `signal_processor.write_signals` with `sign

 - al_type="compliance"`, `source="compliance_scan"`, `source_detail="F9_pilot_{state}"`, and stable `message_id = f"compliance_{state}_{sha1(url+district)[:12]}"` for dedup across re-scans. Auto-

 - queue mode is disabled until ≥60% validation rate is proven over 15+ extractions. Promotion happens via `/signal_act N` which uses the strategy mapping above. Per-state 24h rate limit enforced

 - via module-level `_LAST_SCAN` dict (Commit 5) — override by waiting 24h or restarting Railway.

 156 -

 157 - **`_extract_districts_from_pdf` returns `(items, error_msg_or_none)` tuple** (Session 52 Commit 2). Allows `scan_compliance_gaps` to distinguish "parse failed" from "no districts found in docum

 - ent" — both return empty lists in the first element but the second element is `None` vs an error string. The return dict now surfaces a `parse_errors` list for operator visibility.

 158 -

 159 - **New territory_data helper: `lookup_district_enrollment(name, state) -> int`** (Session 52 Commit 1, refined in Commit 4). Matches on pre-computed `Name Key` column first, then falls back to r

 - e-normalizing `District Name`, then a token-subset pre-check (if target tokens are a strict subset of candidate tokens or vice versa), then `csv_importer.fuzzy_match_name` with threshold 0.7. R

 - eturns 0 on miss. The token-subset pre-check exists because `csv_importer.fuzzy_match_name` has a gap for 1-token-subset-of-multi-token cases (e.g., "carlinville" ⊂ "carlinville 1") — see `memo

 - ry/feedback_fuzzy_match_limits.md`.

 160 -

 161 - **Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call execute_tool() directly.** `/brief`, `/call`, `/recent_calls`, `/progress`, `/sync_activities`, `/call_list`, `/pipeline`, `/pipeline_import`, `

 - /import_closed_lost`, `/import_leads`, `/import_contacts`, `/enrich_leads`, and all `/prospect_*` commands call execute_tool() directly and return. Direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern

 - — when conversation history is long, Claude responds with descriptive text instead of calling tools.

 162 -

 163 - **`/build_sequence` is a hybrid.** Routes through Claude for clarifying questions. But `execute_tool("build_sequence")` sends output via `await send_message()` directly and returns a short ack

 - string to prevent Claude from rewriting.

 164 -

 165 - **`execute_tool` can send directly to Telegram for long outputs.** For tools that return content Claude tends to rewrite, use `await send_message(full_output)` inside `execute_tool` and return

 - a short ack string.

 166 -

 167 - **Suppress `text_response` when tool_calls are present.** Use `if text_response and not tool_calls:` before sending Claude's text. Tool preamble text is noise.

 168 -

 169 - **Never design workflows that require pasting large text through Telegram.** 4,096 char limit. Use fetch-first: Scout reads from GitHub, asks for changes in plain English.

 170 -

 171 - **GAS bridge: new Code.gs edits need a new deployment version.** See `gas/CLAUDE.md` for the full checklist.

 172 -

 173 - **GAS deployment URL does NOT change when bumping version.** Only need to update Railway env var if creating a brand-new deployment (not editing an existing one).

 174 -

 175 - **Active Accounts "Display Name" column is the account name.** Sheet header currently says "Display Name". Steven wants it renamed to "Active Account Name" — this will happen automatically on n

 - ext account CSV import (csv_importer rewrites header). Until then, any code reading active accounts must check BOTH column names: `acc.get("Active Account Name", "") or acc.get("Display Name",

 - "")`. The `territory_data.py` gap analysis already has this fallback.

 176 -

 177 - **Urban Institute Education Data API returns county_code as strings, not integers.** `"6037"` not `6037`. All county code comparisons must use string keys.

 178 -

 179 - **Territory gap coverage: school accounts ≠ district coverage.** Only district-level Active Account deals count as "covered." A school account inside a district is an "upward opportunity," not

 - coverage. Coverage % = district deals / total NCES districts.

 180 -

 181 - **Salesforce CSV: Parent Account = always the district.** Account Name can be district/school/library/company. Parent Account filled → sub-unit under that district. Empty → standalone or top-le

 - vel. One level deep: district → schools.

 182 -

 183 - **CSV import default mode is MERGE (non-destructive).** `/import_clear` switches to clear-and-rewrite for next upload only. `/import_replace_state CA` replaces only that state's rows. `/dedup_a

 - ccounts` uses Name Key + State composite key.

 184 -

 185 - **`get_districts_with_schools()` state key is `"State"` (capital S).** Active Accounts use capital-S. `s.get("state")` returns empty — always use `s.get("State")`.

 186 -

 187 - **CSV importer preserves ALL columns.** Known columns mapped via `_SF_COL_MAP`/`_OPP_COL_MAP`, unknown pass through. Pipeline uses REPLACE ALL (point-in-time snapshot). Always normalize names t

 - o sentence case preserving acronyms (ISD, HS, STEM). CSV uploads decode with utf-8-sig.

 188 -

 189 - **Auto-detect CSV routing by headers.** Priority: pipeline > sf_leads > sf_contacts > accounts. Natural language description or caption overrides auto-detect. Slash commands override everything

 - .

 190 -

 191 - **Pipeline uses 3-tier stale alerts.** 🟠 14+ days, 🟡 30+ days, 🔴 45+ days. Empty Last Activity is NOT flagged.

 192 -

 193 - **Active Accounts "Account Type" column: district | school | library | company.** Old boolean "Is District" column is gone. Do not reintroduce TRUE/FALSE logic.

 194 -

 195 - **Sheet dedup uses email as primary key.** Falls back to `first|last` name for no-email contacts. Don't use name+district — Claude varies district_name spelling.

 196 -

 197 - **Research completion always calls `log_research_job`.** Failure to log is silent.

 198 -

 199 - **`classify_account()` checks district patterns BEFORE school keywords.** "Austin Independent School District" must not match "school" first.

 200 -

 201 - **Name ends in "school" (singular) → school. "schools" (plural) → district.** Explicit rule from Steven.

 202 -

 203 - **District/school names: normalize aggressively, ask when ambiguous.** `normalize_name()` handles abbreviation expansion + suffix stripping. Always normalize both sides before comparing across

 - sources.

 204 -

 205 - **call_processor.py must use claude-sonnet-4-6.** claude-opus-4-5 hangs indefinitely. Anthropic client timeout=90.0.

 206 -

 207 - **`agent/target_roles.py` is the authoritative lead targeting filter.** Contains TIER1/TIER2 titles, CTE relevant/exclude keywords, IT infra exclusions, and `is_relevant_role()` function. Used

 - by `contact_extractor.py` post-extraction. Source: Steven's "ROLES and KEYWORDS" doc.

 208 -

 209 - **Research engine has 20 layers in 5 parallel phases.** Phase A (parallel): L1,L2,L3,L5,L16,L20. Phase B: L4 (domain discovery). Phase C (parallel): L6,L11-L14,L17-L19. Phase D: L7,L8. Phase E:

 - L9→L10→L15→L10. New layers: L16 (Exa broad), L17 (Exa domain-scoped), L18 (Firecrawl extract — needs paid plan), L19 (Firecrawl site map — needs paid plan), L20 (Brave Search).

 210 -

 211 - **Firecrawl paid plan deferred (budget).** L18/L19 skip gracefully when FIRECRAWL_API_KEY has no credits. Was the #1 tool discovery — schema-based extraction pulled 10-20 verified contacts per

 - district. Circle back when budget allows.

 212 -

 213 - **Contact extractor max_tokens is 4000 (not 2000).** School directory pages with 15+ contacts were causing JSON truncation. Fixed Session 40.

 214 -

 215 - **`proximity_engine.py` is a flat module imported at top of main.py.** Targeted + state sweep modes. `add nearby` queues from last results. ESA mapping via `esa [state]`. All direct dispatch. `

 - _last_proximity_result` is in-memory only — lost on bot restart.

 216 -

 217 - **`signal_processor.py` is a flat module imported at top of main.py.** 19 signal sources, 31 Telegram commands, all direct-dispatch. Daily 7:45 AM CST. Weekly Monday: leadership + RFP. Monthly

 - 1st Monday: legislation + grants + budget. On-demand: algebra, cyber, roles, CSTA. BoardDocs/Ballotpedia/RSS wrapped in try/except (non-fatal). `_last_signal_batch` is in-memory — run `/signals

 - ` before `/signal_act` or `/signal_info`.

 218 -

 219 - **Signal enrichment must run before acting on signals.** Never queue a district based on a headline alone. `enrich_signal()` does Serper web search + Claude Haiku analysis for CodeCombat releva

 - nce (strong/moderate/weak/none). A $6.2B bond can be WEAK if it's all devices. A no-dollar STEAM coordinator hire can be STRONG. Auto-runs on Tier 1 during daily scans; manual via `/signal_enri

 - ch N`.

 220 -

 221 - **Google Alerts are weekly digests with all keywords bundled into one email.** Format: `=== News - N new results for [KEYWORD] ===` sections. Parser normalizes `\r\n` to `\n`. `body_limit=65000

 - ` required (digests are ~56K chars). ~80 stories per digest.

 222 -

 223 - **`/signals` defaults to territory-only.** `format_hot_signals(territory_only=True)` filters to 13 states + SoCal. Pass state_filter for single-state view. `_last_signal_batch` is also territor

 - y-filtered.

 224 -

 225 - **Bond/leadership/RFP signals use urgency="time_sensitive" with minimal decay.** Standard Tier 1 decay is 0.93/week. Time-sensitive signals use 0.97/week because the opportunity window matters

 - more than email age.

 226 -

 227 - **Validate email before calling gas.create_draft().** GAS throws on malformed emails.

 228 -

 229 - **`/call_list` must be guarded in the `/call` handler.** `startswith("/call")` matches both. Use `startswith("/call") and not startswith("/call_list")`.

 230 -

 231 - **Call list per-district cap (_MAX_PER_DISTRICT = 2) applies to BOTH priority matches AND backfill.**

 232 -

 233 - **NEVER fabricate claims about active accounts in sequences.** Only cite verifiable facts: school name, license count. No assumed success/engagement.

 234 -

 235 - **ALL sequences are drafts — always show for Steven's approval.** Write to Google Doc, share link in Telegram, mark prospect as "draft" status. Never auto-finalize or auto-mark "complete". Stev

 - en reviews and either approves or gives feedback on changes. This applies to ALL strategies (upward, cold, winback), not just winback.

 236 -

 237 - **Sequence building rules are in `memory/sequence_building_rules.md`.** Load as context when auto-building sequences.

 238 -

 239 - **`scheduler` is a module-level global in main.py.** It must be instantiated at module scope (alongside `memory` and `conversation_history`), not inside `_run_telegram_and_scheduler()`. If it's

 - local to that function, `handle_message()` can't access it and all message handling silently dies. Fixed Session 23.

 240 -

 241 - **`global` declarations go at the TOP of `handle_message()`, not in elif blocks.** Python SyntaxError if `global` appears after first use of the variable. All globals in one line: `global conve

 - rsation_history, _pending_draft, _last_prospect_batch, _pending_approve_force, _csv_import_mode, _csv_import_state, _pipeline_import_mode, _pending_csv_intent, _last_proximity_result`.

 242 -

 243 - **`_on_prospect_research_complete` is the auto-pipeline callback.** Standard flow → strategy-aware sequence → Google Doc → mark complete. `sequence_builder` lazy imported.

 244 -

 245 - **`/prospect_approve` checks Active Accounts before queuing.** Warns on existing district customers. `_pending_approve_force` + `_last_prospect_batch` are in-memory only — lost on restart.

 246 -

 247 - **Two prospecting strategies — upward and cold.** Upward = school accounts, no district deal. Cold = no presence. 8 priority tiers (900+ to 300+). Small/medium above large in same tier.

 248 -

 249 - **No Salesforce or Outreach API access.** All data enters Scout via CSV export or Gmail notification parsing. Never design features assuming API access to Salesforce, Outreach, PandaDoc, or Dia

 - lpad.

 250 -

 251 - **Outreach handoff pattern for cold sequences.** Scout builds content → Google Doc → Steven copy-pastes into Outreach.io. Do NOT try to replace Outreach with Gmail for cold sequences.

 252 -

 253 - **Fireflies Gmail polling uses startup seeding.** First scan adds all existing emails to set without processing. Only post-startup emails trigger workflows.

 254 -

 255 - **`_ensure_tab()` always overwrites the header row.** Column schema changes must propagate immediately.

 256 -

 257 - **Telegram file upload handler is separate.** `handle_document()` uses `filters.Document.ALL`. Never merge into `handle_message`.

 258 -

 259 - **Railway build cache can serve stale code.** If behavior doesn't match, add a logger.info line and check logs after redeploy. If value doesn't appear, trigger manual redeploy.

 260 -

 261 - **After Railway redeploy, wait for "Scout is online" in Telegram before testing.** 409 Conflict errors during ~30s overlap are normal.

 262 -

 CLAUDE.md Trim — Session 58 Pre-Work (v2)

 Context

 CLAUDE.md is 41,817 chars , over the 40K performance ceiling Steven set in Session 53 when docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md was first extracted. It's grown again across Sessions 54–57 via lessons prose, strategy

 internals, and post-mortem narrative. At the same time, the session-start prompt needs two specific things to be visible immediately: the "Session 58 priorities" block and the top session-critical rules.

 Today they sit at line 39 and line 121 respectively — buried under a 900-char Session 57 close paragraph, three lesson sections totaling ~6K chars, and duplicated snapshots of project state that already

 live in SCOUT_PLAN.md and memory/*.md .

 Goal: Trim CLAUDE.md to ~15K chars (not just under 20K — the lower number buys 4–5 sessions of growth room before the next trim) with zero information loss . Every removed line lands in a known, grep-able

 location or is already duplicated elsewhere and can be safely deleted.

 What "zero information loss" means concretely: A distinct-keyword grep audit across the trimmed tree must resolve every rule and fact from the original CLAUDE.md. Nothing relies on human memory or "oh I'll

 remember that lesson."

 What this is not: This is not a content rewrite, a memory cleanup, or a rules deprecation review. It is a physical restructuring — rule text is preserved verbatim where moved, so grep hits on the original

 keywords still resolve. Content-level edits are out of scope except for two deliberate cuts (Session 55/56 lesson prose, and the deprecated "complete replacement files" rule).

 Foundation Check — Why this architecture, not something else

 1. Mild trim (stay near 35–39K). Rejected — the file will cross 40K again in 2 sessions. Sustainable structure requires cutting deeper now and setting up a growth sink (SCOUT_RULES.md) that absorbs future

 rules instead of CLAUDE.md.

 2. Move rules into memory/*.md as feedback entries. Rejected — memory schema is behavioral feedback + user/project state + references, not architectural invariants. MEMORY.md index is near its 200-line load

 cap; adding 60+ entries would blow it. Memory entries also aren't grep-able by topic the way a single sectioned reference file is.

 3. Push rules down into agent/CLAUDE.md and tools/CLAUDE.md (module-scoped). Partial yes, mostly no. Those files are already populated ( agent/CLAUDE.md = 2.6K, tools/CLAUDE.md = 19.7K) with module APIs.

 Adding cross-cutting rules to them mixes two concerns. Cross-cutting rules (plan mode, git push, async safety, signal routing) have no single module home and belong in a project-level reference.

 4. Delete the CRITICAL RULES section entirely and rely on Claude greping when stuck. Rejected — Claude doesn't know what it doesn't know. CLAUDE.md is the canonical "read this at session start" surface; a

 handful of high-severity rules must be visible without grepping for them first.

 Chosen architecture: Three-tier split matching how content gets accessed.

 - Tier 1 (always loaded, CLAUDE.md ~15K): current state + Session 58 priorities + top 15 session-critical rules + pointers.

 - Tier 2 (on-demand, read by section or grep, docs/SCOUT_RULES.md NEW ~22K): full rule set organized by topic, verbatim text from original CLAUDE.md.

 - Tier 3 (already exists, no changes): SCOUT_PLAN.md (active plan + completed features), docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md (repo tree + env vars + tools + commands), SCOUT_HISTORY.md (bug log + changelog),

 memory/*.md (behavioral feedback auto-loaded at session start).

 Deleted content that's duplicated across tiers 2/3: not re-homed, just removed — git history is the archive if ever needed.

 What CLAUDE.md Keeps (Tier 1 content)

 Skeleton (in order):

 1. Header — title + timestamp line

 2. ## CURRENT STATE — session-specific, compressed:

 - Delete: Session 57 close paragraph (900 chars — redundant with "What's working" block below)

 - Compress: "What's working after Session 57" — 9 bullets → one line each (~40% reduction)

 - Keep: "What's in-progress / unresolved" — 5 bullets, unchanged (Session 58 carryover)

 - Delete: "Zero bugs remain" line (200 chars — duplicates Recent Sessions)

 3. ### Recent sessions — 7 one-liners, unchanged

 4. ### What still needs to be done (Session 58) — 5 numbered items, unchanged. This is the session agenda and must remain prominent.

 5. ### Session 57 lessons — compress to one-line takeaways (6 lessons × ~60 chars = 400 chars, down from ~6K). Full prose moves to SCOUT_RULES.md under an "Appendix: Session 57 post-mortem" section so grep

 still resolves.

 6. ## CRITICAL RULES — trimmed to top-15 (see list below)

 7. ## WHAT SCOUT IS — unchanged (architecture + communication, already lean)

 8. ## REFERENCE MATERIAL — updated to list both docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md and new docs/SCOUT_RULES.md with topic hints

 All of these are duplicated elsewhere. git log is the fallback if anything is ever missed.

 Expected CLAUDE.md after trim: 41.8K − 27K ≈ 15K chars. Leaves ~25K of headroom before hitting 40K again — ~4–5 sessions of growth.

 What Gets Lost from Session 55/56 Lessons (deliberate)

 Of 13 total Session 55/56 lesson bullets, 5 are already in memory/ and 3 move to SCOUT_RULES.md (see next section). The remaining 5 are deliberately dropped to git history:

 - "Every plan is one-shot" (Session 56) — already in memory/feedback_plans_are_one_shot.md ✓

 - "Filter at earliest boundary" (Session 55) — already in memory/feedback_filter_upstream_not_downstream.md ✓

 - "Don't ask permission on verified capabilities" (Session 55) — already in memory/feedback_take_initiative_on_verified_capabilities.md ✓

 - "Serper snippets flow into raw_pages" (Session 56) — already in memory/reference_serper_snippets_as_raw_pages.md ✓

 - "Verify foundational assumptions before plans" (Session 56) — covered by memory/feedback_plans_are_one_shot.md + pressure-test pattern ✓

 - "Single source of truth helpers" (Session 55) — general DRY, drop

 - "Known-bad oracle not sufficient" (Session 55) — validation-harness-specific, drop (git log if needed)

 - "_ensure_headers vs _ensure_tab confusion" (Session 55) — one-time correction, already fixed in code

 - "Canonicalize both sides for district lookup" (Session 56) — move to SCOUT_RULES.md §4

 - "L6 scrape blind to modern CMS" (Session 56) — move to SCOUT_RULES.md §8

 - "Queue slash command naming inconsistent" (Session 56) — reference fact, drop (git log if needed)

 - "L15 bypasses L9 validation" (Session 55) — move to SCOUT_RULES.md §8

 - "Railway log API DateTime scalar" (Session 55) — move to SCOUT_RULES.md §13

 - "Scout research can hang 20+ min" (Session 55) — move to SCOUT_RULES.md §13

 Anything needed later but not re-homed? git show HEAD:CLAUDE.md | grep -A 2 "keyword" resolves from git history in one command. That's the safety net.

 Top-15 Rules for CLAUDE.md

 Selection criterion: (frequency of violation) × (severity when violated). Re-examined from the original 80-rule list:

 1. Always enter plan mode before non-trivial builds (Session 51 shipped 7 features without plan mode; 3 had BLOCKER bugs)

 2. Always push code from Claude Code via git , never /push_code in Telegram (4096 char truncation)

 3. Read before write — read the module file first. Module APIs in agent/CLAUDE.md + tools/CLAUDE.md

 4. Every plan is one-shot; pressure-test hard BEFORE presenting (Session 56 rule)

 5. Verify foundational assumptions empirically BEFORE writing plans (Session 57 rule: both BUG 1 + BUG 2 had rev-1 silent bugs that only surfaced via Serper/httpx probes)

 6. New scanners ship with kill switches — ENABLE_X_SCAN = True constant near top of tools/signal_processor.py

 7. Multi-feature sessions → one commit per feature (surgical git revert )

 8. Signal vs Prospect routing: HIGH confidence → district_prospector.add_district() as pending ; MEDIUM/LOW → write_signals() ; active customer match → customer_intel log only

 9. handle_message() and async tasks must call get_gas_bridge() locally — never reference gas as free variable ( Unbound Local Error trap, shipped twice in Session 49)

 10. No requests or time.sleep() in async functions — use httpx.AsyncClient + await asyncio.sleep()

 11. global declarations go at the TOP of handle_message() — Python SyntaxError if they appear after first use

 12. tool_result always follows tool_use — wrap every execute_tool() call in try/except; missing → 400 on next API call

 13. Explicit slash commands bypass Claude and call execute_tool() directly — direct dispatch is the only reliable pattern when context is long

 14. NEVER fabricate claims in sequences; ALL sequences are drafts — always Google Doc + Telegram link for Steven's approval

 15. Never design workflows requiring large-text paste through Telegram (4096 char limit) — fetch-first pattern via GitHub

 Pointer line (16th entry):

 ▎ Full rule set (GAS bridge, CSV import, research engine, signal processor internals, priority scoring, Outreach API gotchas, data model invariants, deployment ops): docs/SCOUT_RULES.md — read by section or

 ▎ grep by keyword.

 - "Complete replacement files, never partial snippets" — outdated (workflow now uses Edit/Write tools directly, not chat-message file dumps). Still lives in SCOUT_RULES.md §1 for historical record.

 docs/SCOUT_RULES.md Structure

 Format: Mirrors docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md — extraction header, section headers, bold lead sentences preserved verbatim from original CLAUDE.md so grep on existing keywords still resolves.

 Header template:

 # SCOUT — Full Rules Reference

 This file holds the full rule set. CLAUDE.md keeps the top 15 session-critical

 rules inline; everything else lives here. Read by section or grep by keyword —

 every rule's original bolded lead sentence is preserved verbatim.

 - `docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md` — repo tree, env vars, tools, commands

 - `CLAUDE.md` — current state, Session N priorities, top 15 rules

 - `SCOUT_PLAN.md` — active plan + completed feature notes

 - `memory/*.md` — behavioral feedback, auto-loaded

 ---

 Sections (13 total):

 1. Workflow & Process (rules already in top-15 mirrored here as section anchors so grep still hits: plan mode, git push, read-before-write, one-shot plans, empirical verification, complete replacement files

 as deprecated note, pressure-test loop)

 2. Python & Async Safety ( run_in_executor , global declarations, scheduler module-level global, synchronous code from async)

 3. Architecture & Module Imports (lazy imports for Phase 4+, top-level imports for flat modules, _on_prospect_research_complete callback pattern, tool_result follows tool_use )

 4. Priority Scoring & Strategy Internals (full _calculate_priority strategy table formatted as actual markdown table — NOT prose; add_district(**kwargs) size metadata; _SIGNAL_TYPE_TO_STRATEGY dict; F9

 pilot mode + _LAST_SCAN rate limit; _extract_districts_from_pdf tuple return; lookup_district_enrollment helper; /prospect_approve active-account check; upward/cold two-strategy model; district name

 canonicalization for enrichment lookups — from Session 56 diocesan lesson)

 5. Data Model, CSV Import & Sheets Schema (Display Name / Active Account Name dual-read; CSV merge default; importer preserves all columns; auto-detect routing; _SF_COL_MAP / _OPP_COL_MAP ; pipeline 3-tier

 stale alerts; classify_account district-before-school; school vs schools rule; normalize_name ; Account Type column values; email-primary-key dedup; get_districts_with_schools state-key capitalization; Urban

 Institute county_code strings; Salesforce Parent Account semantics; territory gap coverage rule; _ensure_tab always overwrites header; handle_document separate handler)

 6. GAS Bridge ( createDraft + skip_if_draft_exists=True ; Code.gs edits need deployment version bump; deployment URL stable; validate_email before gas.create_draft )

 7. Research Engine & Contact Extraction (20 layers / 5 phases topology; target_roles.py authoritative filter; contact_extractor max_tokens=4000; Firecrawl paid plan deferred; log_research_job completion

 requirement; L6 direct scrape blind to modern CMS from Session 56; L15 additions bypass L9 validation from Session 55; Serper snippets flow into raw_pages via _add_raw_from_serper from Session 56 memory)

 8. Signal Processor (19 sources / 31 direct-dispatch commands; daily/weekly/monthly schedule; enrich_signal() mandatory before acting; Google Alerts digest parsing + body_limit=65000 ; /signals

 territory-only default; time-sensitive urgency decay 0.97/week; _last_signal_batch in-memory)

 9. Telegram & Command Dispatch ( /call_list guard in /call handler via startswith; _MAX_PER_DISTRICT = 2 ; suppress text_response when tool_calls present; execute_tool direct-send for long outputs;

 /build_sequence hybrid routing pattern; call_processor.py must use claude-sonnet-4-6 not opus)

 10. Sequence Building & Content Rules ( sequence_building_rules.md context load; anti-fabrication rule; all-drafts rule verbatim from current CLAUDE.md)

 11. Outreach.io API Gotchas (Steven user ID 11; interval in seconds; to Recipients: [] empty; creation order sequence→steps→templates→link→activate→prospects; <br><br> in body Html; schedules UI-only; C4

 sequence/schedule/template IDs 1995–1998 / 50 / 43784)

 12. Design Constraints (No Salesforce/Outreach/PandaDoc/Dialpad API for data entry; Outreach handoff pattern = Google Doc → copy-paste; Fireflies Gmail polling startup seeding)

 13. Operations & Deployment (Railway build cache stale-code workaround; wait for "Scout is online" post-redeploy; 409 Conflict rolling-deploy overlap normal; Railway log API DateTime scalar + 5000-line

 limit from Session 55; Scout research can hang 20+ min then recover from Session 55)

 Appendix A: Session 57 lessons — full prose preserved (so CLAUDE.md can keep only one-line takeaways in Tier 1).

 Appendix B: Scanner Pattern — URL preservation / source_url schema / vendor site queries fail (cross-refs to memory/feedback_scanner_url_preservation.md + feedback_vendor_site_queries_fail.md).

 Execution Sequence

 Ordered so that the highest-leverage decisions happen first, verification happens before declaring done, and any failure at a step reverts cleanly.

 Step 1 — Pre-flight reads (duplication check)

 # Confirm no rule I'm about to move to SCOUT_RULES.md already lives in a module-scoped file

 grep -l "_calculate_priority\|add_district\|enrich_with_csta\|signal_processor\|research_engine\|classify_account" \

 /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/agent/CLAUDE.md \

 /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/tools/CLAUDE.md

 If any of those hit — read the match context and ensure SCOUT_RULES.md doesn't repeat API-level detail. Module files stay authoritative for signatures; SCOUT_RULES.md has cross-cutting invariants.

 Also re-read full CLAUDE.md (in chunks) one more time to confirm no section I haven't listed above is missed.

 Step 2 — Write docs/SCOUT_RULES.md (full file)

 Single Write call. Sections 1–13 + Appendices A–B. Every rule's original bolded lead sentence preserved verbatim — this is the grep-audit contract. Format _calculate_priority strategies as a table for

 readability. Collapse the two overlapping add_district / _calculate_priority paragraphs into one entry.

 Step 3 — Edit docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md

 Append a new ## SESSION WORKFLOW section at the end, containing the current ### Session transcript capture block from CLAUDE.md verbatim (7 bullets).

 Step 4 — Write new CLAUDE.md (full file replace)

 Single Write call. Skeleton per "What CLAUDE.md Keeps" section. Char-count target: 14–16K.

 Step 5 — Char-count gate

 wc -c /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/CLAUDE.md /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/docs/SCOUT_RULES.md /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md

 Expect: CLAUDE.md < 17,000; SCOUT_RULES.md 20,000–25,000; SCOUT_REFERENCE.md ~14,500.

 If CLAUDE.md > 17,000: identify biggest remaining block (probably compressed "What's working" or top-15 rules) and trim further. Don't ship over-budget.

 Step 6 — Grep audit (no information loss gate)

 Concrete bash loop. Every distinctive keyword from the original CLAUDE.md must resolve in at least one downstream file.

 cd /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout

 KEYWORDS=(

 # Process

 "plan mode" "push_code" "Read before write" "complete replacement"

 "kill switch" "one commit per feature" "pressure-test"

 # Async/Python traps

 "get_gas_bridge" "Unbound Local Error" "httpx.Async Client" "run_in_executor"

 "global.*handle_message" "scheduler.*module-level"

 # Tool contract

 "tool_result" "tool_use" "Suppress.*text_response" "execute_tool.*directly"

 # Priority scoring

 "_calculate_priority" "add_district" "_SIGNAL_TYPE_TO_STRATEGY"

 "compliance_gap" "_LAST_SCAN" "_extract_districts_from_pdf"

 "lookup_district_enrollment" "Name Key" "fuzzy_match_name"

 "upward" "winback" "proximity" "intra_district" "cs_funding_recipient"

 "competitor_displacement" "csta_partnership" "charter_cmo" "cte_center"

 "private_school_network" "homeschool_coop"

 # Data model

 "Display Name" "Active Account Name" "Account Type"

 "classify_account" "normalize_name" "Parent Account" "county_code"

 "_ensure_tab" "_SF_COL_MAP" "_OPP_COL_MAP" "utf-8-sig"

 "get_districts_with_schools" "3-tier stale" "handle_document"

 # GAS

 "create Draft" "skip_if_draft_exists" "thread Has Draft" "deployment version"

 "validate_email"

 # Research engine

 "20 layers" "target_roles" "TIER1" "is_relevant_role"

 "contact_extractor.*4000" "Firecrawl.*deferred" "log_research_job"

 "_add_raw_from_serper" "L6.*blind" "L15.*L9"

 # Signal processor

 "signal_processor" "19 signal sources" "enrich_signal"

 "Google Alerts.*digest" "body_limit" "territory_only" "0.97"

 # Telegram

 "_MAX_PER_DISTRICT" "/call_list.*guard" "build_sequence.*hybrid"

 "call_processor.*sonnet"

 # Sequence content

 "NEVER fabricate" "ALL sequences are drafts" "sequence_building_rules"

 # Outreach API

 "user ID.*11" "interval.*seconds" "toRecipients.*\[\]"

 "C4 sequences.*1995" "Schedule.*50" "template.*43784"

 # Design constraints

 "No Salesforce or Outreach API" "Outreach handoff" "Fireflies.*startup"

 # Ops

 "Railway build cache" "Scout is online" "409 Conflict"

 "DateTime scalar" "Scout.*hang"

 # Priority scoring edge cases

 "functools.partial" "est_enrollment" "school_count" "total_licenses"

 # Session 57 lessons preserved verbatim

 "temperature=0" "static finite directories" "_load_csta_roster"

 # Session 56 diocesan

 "_canonical_diocesan_key" "ENABLE_DIOCESAN_PLAYBOOK" "_target_match_params"

 )

 MISSING=()

 for kw in "${KEYWORDS[@]}" ; do

 if ! grep -qE "$kw" \

 CLAUDE.md \

 docs/SCOUT_RULES.md \

 docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md \

 SCOUT_PLAN.md \

 SCOUT_HISTORY.md \

 /Users/stevenadkins/.claude/projects/-Users-stevenadkins-Code-Scout/memory/*.md \

 2>/dev/null; then

 MISSING+=( "$kw" )

 done

 if [ ${#MISSING[@]} -eq 0 ]; then

 echo "GREP AUDIT PASS — all ${#KEYWORDS[@]} keywords resolve"

 else

 echo "GREP AUDIT FAIL — ${#MISSING[@]} keywords missing:"

 printf ' %s\n' "${MISSING[@]}"

 Step 7 — Git diff eyeball

 git diff -- stat CLAUDE.md docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md

 git diff CLAUDE.md | head -200

 Look for: unexpected deletions, markdown formatting breakage, empty sections, stale cross-references (e.g., "see SCOUT_REFERENCE.md" pointing at a section that no longer exists).

 Step 8 — Report + handoff to Session 58

 Report to Steven:

 - Final char counts (CLAUDE.md before/after, SCOUT_RULES.md new, SCOUT_REFERENCE.md delta)

 - Grep audit result (pass/fail count)

 - Any dropped-to-git-history lessons (explicit list of the 5 Session 55/56 bullets that weren't re-homed)

 - Confirmation: Session 58 priorities now visible within first ~20 lines of CLAUDE.md

 No commit yet — Steven reviews the trim in working tree before committing. Per project rule, Claude only commits when explicitly asked.

 Critical Files

 - /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/CLAUDE.md — full rewrite via Write

 - /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/docs/SCOUT_RULES.md — NEW via Write

 - /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md — append-only Edit

 Edge Cases & Gotchas

 1. Stale cross-references. Original CLAUDE.md has lines like "see agent/CLAUDE.md and tools/CLAUDE.md" and "details in SCOUT_PLAN.md + SCOUT_HISTORY.md". These must survive in the trimmed version or get

 updated to point at SCOUT_RULES.md where applicable.

 2. Markdown formatting in the _calculate_priority table. Converting from prose to table is a content-shape change, not just a move. Must render correctly — pipe characters and column alignment.

 3. Top-15 rule ordering. Keep severity-ordered (plan mode first because it's the highest-leverage process rule). Don't alphabetize.

 4. CLAUDE.md section ordering. Put ## CURRENT STATE → What still needs to be done before ## CRITICAL RULES . Session-start prompt reads top-down; agenda must hit before rules.

 5. docs/SCOUT_RULES.md grep contract. Every original bolded lead sentence must appear verbatim. Do not paraphrase. If I reword a rule, grep on the original phrasing fails silently and the audit misses it

 because the keyword list is itself seeded from the original.

 6. Appendix A (Session 57 lesson prose) inside SCOUT_RULES.md. Keep lesson headers as exact phrases from CLAUDE.md so the keyword audit hits them.

 7. Session numbering header in CLAUDE.md. *Last updated: 2026-04-11 — End of Session 57 (...)* — keep as-is. The scout_session.sh wrapper parses this for session numbering. Do not rename or restructure the

 title line.

 Verification Summary

 - Char-count gate: CLAUDE.md < 17K chars (target 14–16K)

 - Grep audit gate: 0 missing keywords from the curated list above

 - Visual gate: Session 58 priorities visible in first ~20 lines of CLAUDE.md

 - Markdown gate: Trimmed CLAUDE.md renders without formatting errors (no dangling code fences, broken tables, or orphan links)

 - Cross-ref gate: Every pointer in trimmed CLAUDE.md resolves to a file that actually exists with the referenced content

 Only after all 5 gates pass do I report done and hand off to Session 58 priorities.

 Key Changes from v1 (for Steven's review)

 1. Delete Session 57 close paragraph instead of compressing. v1 said "compress to one sentence." v2: it's fully redundant with the "What's working" block that immediately follows. Deletion is cleaner and

 saves 900 chars.

 2. Compress Session 57 lessons to one-line takeaways; full prose → SCOUT_RULES.md Appendix A. v1 kept all 6 lessons verbatim in CLAUDE.md — that's 6K chars, 40% of the trimmed budget. v2 keeps one-liners

 (~400 chars) in CLAUDE.md, full prose in SCOUT_RULES.md Appendix A with original headers preserved for grep.

 3. Delete Session 55/56 lessons entirely; no SCOUT_HISTORY.md append. v1 moved non-memory lessons to SCOUT_HISTORY.md. v2 rationale: git history archives CLAUDE.md cheaply ( git show HEAD:CLAUDE.md ). Of 13

 bullets, 5 are already in memory, 3 move to SCOUT_RULES.md where they're operationally relevant, and 5 drop to git history. SCOUT_HISTORY.md is for bugs + changelog — lesson post-mortems don't fit.

 4. Move Session transcript capture to docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md. v1 kept it in CLAUDE.md. v2: it's static workflow reference that matches SCOUT_REFERENCE.md's existing "static reference extracted from

 CLAUDE.md" framing. Saves ~1K chars.

 5. Top-15 list refined. v1 listed 15 rules but included "static finite directories are lookups" (a lesson, already in memory) and "complete replacement files" (deprecated workflow rule from the

 pre-Edit/Write-tool era). v2 drops both; promotes " global declarations at top of handle_message" (Python SyntaxError trap) and "Explicit slash commands bypass Claude" (fundamental to Scout's dispatch

 model).

 6. Grep audit is concrete. v1 said "grep each keyword" with 18 examples. v2 provides an executable bash loop with 80+ keywords and a pass/fail gate. Without an executable audit, "no information loss" is an

 unchecked claim.

 7. Pre-flight step added (Step 1). v1 assumed agent/CLAUDE.md + tools/CLAUDE.md don't overlap with new content. v2 greps them first to confirm. Cost: 30 seconds. Benefit: no duplication if I'm wrong about

 their scope.

 8. Target char count tightened from <20K to 14–16K. v1's 20K target leaves 2 sessions of growth before re-trim. v2's 15K leaves 4–5 sessions. Trimming once and doing it right is cheaper than trimming twice.

 9. _calculate_priority as a table in SCOUT_RULES.md. v1 moved the strategy list as prose. v2 formats as a markdown table. Strategy/priority-range/size-kwarg data is tabular; prose obscures it.

 10. Deferred tool_result decision moved from prose to top-15. v1 left "tool_result always follows tool_use" in SCOUT_RULES.md. v2 promotes to top-15 — it's a hard API contract that causes 400 errors on the

 next call if missed, which is exactly the "silent-bug-prone" criterion that distinguishes Tier 1 from Tier 2.

 11. No SCOUT_HISTORY.md edit at all. v1 had it as one of four files touched. v2 removes it from the file map. Fewer files touched = fewer things to get wrong.

 The biggest single improvement from v1 → v2 is the realization that most of the v1 plan's "safety moves" (move Session 55/56 lessons to SCOUT_HISTORY.md, keep Session 57 lessons verbatim in CLAUDE.md) were

 preserving material that either isn't load-bearing for future sessions or already exists in memory/ + git . v1 over-indexed on "don't delete anything." v2 accepts that git history is a real archive and stops

 pretending every 2-sentence post-mortem needs a permanent home.

 Second biggest: executable verification . v1's grep audit was aspirational. v2's is a bash loop with a pass/fail gate. If the audit fails, I fix and re-run before declaring done — that's the actual

 zero-info-loss guarantee.

 Third: target tightening . The whole point of a trim is to buy multiple sessions of growth runway. 20K target bought 2; 15K buys 5. Same work, 2.5× the useful lifespan.

 204 + *Moved from CLAUDE.md during Session 58 trim. The `scout` wrapper and transcript pipeline are static workflow reference, not active rules.*

 205 +

 206 + - Steven starts sessions with `scout` in terminal (not `claude`). This wraps Claude Code with `script` to capture full transcripts.

 207 + - Transcripts save to `docs/sessions/session_N.md`. Raw captures in `docs/sessions/.raw/` (gitignored).

 208 + - During end-of-session routine: run `python3 scripts/clean_transcript.py "$SCOUT_RAW_TRANSCRIPT" "$SCOUT_CLEAN_TRANSCRIPT"`, then include the clean file in the commit.

 209 + - Env vars available: `SCOUT_SESSION_NUM`, `SCOUT_RAW_TRANSCRIPT`, `SCOUT_CLEAN_TRANSCRIPT`.

 210 + - Steven uses `/exit` between sessions (not `/clear`) so each `scout` run = one transcript file.

 211 + - Wrapper script auto-commits as fallback if end-of-session routine is skipped.

 212 + - **Session numbering source of truth is CLAUDE.md header** (`Session N`). The `scout_session.sh` script parses this, transcript files on disk, and raw files — uses the highest. This prevents d

 + rift when sessions run without the `scout` wrapper.

 44 + - Consider diocesan email verification upgrade (paid Apollo/RocketReach/Hunter, or L8 pattern inference seeding)

 50 - ### Session 57 lessons (bank these)

 51 - - **Static finite directories are lookups, not scanners.** CSTA chapters, Catholic dioceses, charter CMOs, CTE centers — any data source that's bounded and changes less than weekly should be fe

 - tched once into `memory/*.json` and exposed as an enrichment helper, not scheduled as a daily Serper+Haiku scanner. Saved as `memory/feedback_static_directories_are_lookups.md`. Generalizes the

 - BUG 4 diocesan lesson + retires F5 + codifies the pattern for future sources.

 52 - - **Haiku extractions in validation harnesses need `temperature=0.0`.** Without it the gate flip-flops between runs — same snapshot, different HIGH/MEDIUM splits, oracle labeling breaks. BUG 1

 - harness was non-deterministic until I pinned temperature. Saved as `memory/feedback_haiku_temperature_zero_for_gates.md`.

 53 - - **Empirical Serper probes BEFORE plan mode.** Both BUG 1 and BUG 2 plans had silent-bug rev-1s that only surfaced when I ran actual Serper queries against the proposed query templates. BUG 1

 - rev-1 used `"<State>"` quoted which killed yield to 0; rev-2 used unquoted state and yield jumped to 10+. BUG 2 rev-1 was snippet-only which would have yielded 0 because snippets are 200 chars;

 - rev-2 added httpx + BeautifulSoup direct fetch and yield jumped to 14. Rule: don't write a plan on query design without running the queries first.

 54 - - **Browser User-Agent is OK for one-shot local scripts.** Session 56 rule was "don't change Scout's global UA because it touches every public district run." That rule still holds. But a per-sc

 - ript fetcher that runs manually (`scripts/fetch_csta_roster.py`) can use `Mozilla/5.0 …` safely because it's not in the production path. BUG 2 fetcher uses this and succeeds on all csteachers.o

 - rg subdomains.

 55 - - **Eager-load lookup indexes at module import time.** `signal_processor.py`'s `_load_csta_roster()` runs at import via `_load_csta_roster()` call at module bottom. No lazy-init race. Scout imp

 - orts are module-level, one import per process. Lazy patterns are overkill for read-only JSON lookups.

 56 - - **Pressure-test catches silent bugs that would have shipped.** BUG 1 rev-1 had `"<State> State Board of Education"` as a generic template — broken for TX (TEA), CA (CDE), OH (ODE). BUG 2 rev-

 - 1 had snippet-only extraction + wrong dedup key + unquoted-state bug. Both rev-1s would have shipped and produced near-zero yield with no obvious signal. The 7-step pressure test turned both in

 - to working rev-2s on the first pass. Rule: when Steven asks for the pressure test, actually HOLD THE FULL PLAN IN HEAD before reacting — surface all the silent bugs in one pass, rewrite from sc

 - ratch with everything baked in.

 46 + ### Session 57 lessons (full prose in `docs/SCOUT_RULES.md` Appendix A)

 47 + - Static finite directories are **lookups, not scanners** (CSTA, dioceses, CTE, charters → `memory/*.json` + enrichment helper, not daily scan).

 48 + - Haiku extractions in validation harnesses need `temperature=0.0` or the gate flip-flops between runs.

 49 + - **Empirical Serper/httpx probes BEFORE plan mode** — both BUG 1 and BUG 2 had silent rev-1s that only surfaced via live probing.

 50 + - Browser User-Agent is OK for one-shot local scripts (not Scout's global UA).

 51 + - **Eager-load lookup indexes at module import time** — lazy patterns are overkill for read-only JSON.

 52 + - Pressure-test only catches silent bugs if you HOLD THE FULL PLAN IN HEAD before reacting.

 58 - ### Session 56 lessons (bank these)

 59 - - **Every plan is a one-shot.** Don't write a plan expecting Steven to do refining rounds. Self-pressure-test hard; ship the best possible plan first time. Saved as `memory/feedback_plans_are_o

 - ne_shot.md`. Steven explicitly rejected v1 of the BUG 4 plan that ended with "ready for your 7-step pressure test" — he considered that lazy framing that pushed rigor onto him.

 60 - - **Before writing any plan, verify the foundational assumption empirically.** For BUG 4, v1 and v2 assumed L6 direct scrape would contribute to diocesan yield. Ten minutes of `curl` + Beautifu

 - l Soup revealed that Archdiocese of Chicago runs on Liferay + React and Beautiful Soup sees zero text. v3 dropped `DIOCESAN_CENTRAL_PATHS` entirely and refocused the playbook on search-layer quer

 - y tuning. Rule: walk the pipeline in your head end-to-end and ask "what has to be true for this to work?" then go verify each load-bearing assumption.

 61 - - **Serper snippets flow into `raw_pages` via `_add_raw_from_serper`.** For JS-rendered sites, search-index layers (L1/L2/L4/L11/L16/L17/L20) are where contact yield comes from. Don't waste tim

 - e on L6 seed URLs for React/Liferay/Drupal SPAs. Saved as `memory/reference_serper_snippets_as_raw_pages.md`.

 62 - - **When activating a feature via district name lookup, canonicalize both sides.** `_canonical_diocesan_key` lowercases and strips "schools"/"catholic schools" suffix. Both map keys (at build t

 - ime) and lookup input (at runtime) go through the same helper — so "Archdiocese of Chicago Schools", "archdiocese of chicago", and "Archdiocese of Chicago Catholic Schools" all resolve. Rule: n

 - ever trust that users/bots will type the district name the way the seed has it stored.

 63 - - **Single source of truth helpers prevent filter leaks.** Commit C of BUG 4 collapsed 4 inline `target_host/target_hint` computations into a single `_target_match_params()` call. The diocesan

 - override is in one place; non-diocesan behavior is preserved exactly. Post-commit grep gate (`grep 'self\._district_name_hint(self\.district_name)' | count == 1`) confirms no stale sites remain

 - . Rule: when a matching rule needs a per-context override, refactor all call sites behind a single helper FIRST, then add the override.

 64 - - **L6 direct scrape is architecturally blind to modern school CMS platforms.** Liferay, Drupal SPA, Word Press-React, Next.js SPA — Beautiful Soup sees JS shell with zero text. 4 of 16 diocesan

 - domains also WAF-block Scout's `ScoutBot/2.0` user agent outright (Boston, Pittsburgh, OKC, Galveston-Houston). Upgrading Scout's global UA is risky — it touches every public district run. Acce

 - pt L6 as dead weight on diocesan and rely on Serper for yield.

 65 - - **Queue operation slash command naming is inconsistent.** `/prospect_private_networks` (not `/queue_private_school_networks`) is the actual command for `queue_private_school_networks()`. The

 - command registry in `main.py` line ~3508 uses `prospect_` prefixes for seed-queueing functions. Session 56 wasted a command round-trip guessing. Reference the agent/main.py handler block before

 - firing slash commands that aren't in the Scout reference.

 66 -

 67 - ### Session 55 lessons (bank these)

 68 - - **Filter at the earliest boundary that has the signal, not at the output.** BUG 5 plan v2 filtered after Claude extraction (wasted tokens). v3 filters at `raw_pages` boundary before Claude se

 - es the pages. Saved as `memory/feedback_filter_upstream_not_downstream.md`. Walk the pipeline backwards in plan mode and find the earliest stage where the needed signal first becomes available.

 69 - - **Don't ask permission on verified capabilities.** Steven: "you dont need to ask to do that you should have just done it" (re: testing screencapture). Asking on safe reversible known-working

 - actions burns latency. Saved as `memory/feedback_take_initiative_on_verified_capabilities.md`. Still ask on destructive ops, ambiguous decisions, or money-spending.

 70 - - **Shared helpers as single source of truth.** BUG 5 has 4 callers of the same matching logic (Stage 1, Stage 2, strengthened L10, audit script). One helper, four callers. DRY prevents the "4

 - places with subtly different rules" failure mode.

 71 - - **Known-bad oracle is necessary but not sufficient.** 2-row oracle passes trivially for almost any rule. Add a 20-row "must be kept" clean spot-check as a second gate. Both gates block the au

 - dit script from producing a report until they pass.

 72 - - **L15 additions bypass anything that runs in L9.** Any post-L9 validation must ALSO run on L15's `_merge_contacts` sites (two of them). The second L10 pass is a weaker gate than a Stage 2 cal

 - l.

 73 - - **`sheets_writer._ensure_headers` was NOT the auto-migrating function I assumed from root CLAUDE.md.** Root CLAUDE.md's "`_ensure_tab` always overwrites" refers to a different function in `di

 - strict_prospector.py`. Now fixed: `_ensure_headers` auto-migrates when current header is a prefix of expected.

 74 - - **Railway log API queries need Date Time scalar type** (not String). `start Date: Date Time` / `end Date: Date Time`. 5000-line limit per query — narrow the window if near the cap.

 75 - - **Scout bot research task can occasionally hang silently for 1 request then recover.** Session 55 second smoke test hung for 20+ min with zero progress logs, then a third attempt completed in

 - 7 minutes. Possibly a 409 Conflict overlap during rolling deploy. If research seems stuck, wait 2-3 min and retry; don't assume a code bug first.

 91 - ### Outreach.io API (key gotchas — full details in SCOUT_PLAN.md + memory)

 92 - - Steven's user ID: **11**. Write access for sequences/steps/templates/prospects only.

 93 - - **Interval is in SECONDS** (not minutes). 5 min=300, 4 days=345600, 6 days=518400.

 94 - - **`to Recipients` MUST be `[]` (empty).** Never `["{{to Recipient}}"]` — causes all emails to fail.

 95 - - Creation flow: create sequence → steps → templates → link → Steven activates in UI → THEN add prospects.

 96 - - Use `<br><br>` between paragraphs in bodyHtml, NOT `<p>` tags.

 97 - - Schedules: UI-only (no API scopes). Three standard: "Teacher Tue-Thu Multi-Window", "Admin Mon-Thurs Multi-Window", "Hot Lead Mon-Fri".

 98 - - C4 sequences: IDs 1995-1998. Schedule: "C4 Tue-Thu Morning" (ID 50). Steven's template: ID 43784.

 100 - ### Email Reply Drafting (auto + manual)

 101 - **Auto-drafting (Railway):** `tools/email_drafter.py` polls unread inbox every 5 min during business hours (7 AM - 6 PM CST, weekdays). Classifies via Claude Haiku (DRAFT/FLAG/SKIP), drafts via

 - Claude Sonnet with voice profile + playbook + **full thread history** (via `gas.get_threads_bulk`), creates threaded HTML drafts via GAS bridge. Notifies Steven in Telegram. Manual triggers: `

 - /draft_emails`, `/draft force`, `/draft [name]` (targeted — bypasses classification).

 102 - **Claude Code workflow:** When Steven says "draft my emails" in Claude Code, load and follow `prompts/reply_draft.md`.

 103 - Voice rules in `memory/voice_profile.md`. Response patterns in `memory/response_playbook.md`.

 104 - Draft log in `memory/draft_log.md`. Known issue: Outreach browser extension strips draft body for contacts in Outreach.
