 199 + m = re.match( r"https?://([a-z]+)\.csteachers\.org" , url.lower())

 215 + def bucket_urls_by_state ( urls: set [ str ] ) -> tuple [ dict [ str , list [ str ]], list [ str ]]:

 216 + """Partition URLs into state buckets + a national bucket.

 217 + Returns (state_buckets, national_list). """

 172 - HAIKU_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract CSTA (Computer Science Teachers Association) state and regional CHAPTER board members and officers from these pages.

 234 + PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract CSTA (Computer Science Teachers Association) chapter board members and officers from these pages.

 235 +

 236 + CONTEXT: These pages are specifically for the CSTA {state_name} chapter (state code: {state_code}). Default `state` to "{state_code}" for every person unless the page EXPLICITLY says the person

 + teaches in a different state.

 237 +

 238 For each person, return JSON:

 239 {{

 240 "name" : "Full name" ,

 177 - "role": "Chapter role — e.g. 'President', 'Vice President', 'Secretary', 'K-12 Board Member', 'Treasurer'",

 178 - "chapter": "CSTA chapter name — e.g. 'CSTA Pittsburgh ', 'CSTA Longwood ' , 'CSTA Arizona' ",

 179 - "state": "2-letter state code of their K-12 SCHOOL EMPLOYER, NOT their chapter's state (usually the same, but not always)",

 241 + "role" : "Chapter role — e.g. 'President', 'Vice President', 'Secretary', 'K-12 Board Member', 'Treasurer' , 'Chapter Leader' " ,

 242 + "chapter" : "CSTA chapter name — e.g. 'CSTA {state_name} ', 'CSTA Pittsburgh '" ,

 243 + "state" : "2-letter state code — MUST default to '{state_code}' unless page says otherwise" ,

 244 "district" : "Exact K-12 school district name where this person works. Empty string '' if the page does not explicitly name a district — do NOT guess, do NOT use the chapter city as a district

 , do NOT fabricate." ,

 245 "source_url" : "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input that mentions this person. Do not fabricate."

 246 }}

 248 STRICT RULES:

 185 - - Only STATE or REGIONAL chapter officers/board members. SKIP the national

 186 - CSTA Board of Directors (they are too disconnected from any single district).

 249 - SKIP university professors and college advisors — K- 12 only.

 188 - - SKIP general CSTA staff / HQ employees unless they also hold a state /regional

 189 - chapter role.

 250 + - SKIP general CSTA national HQ staff unless they ALSO hold this state chapter role.

 251 - SKIP conference speakers unless explicitly listed as chapter officers.

 191 - - `state` is the employer state. A person on the CSTA Chicago chapter whose

 192 - teaching job is in Gary, Indiana gets state="IN".

 193 - - `district` must be a real named K-12 LEA. If the page says "high school

 194 - computer science teacher" without naming the district, return `district: ""`.

 312 + def build_state_corpus ( state_code: str , urls: list [ str ], linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ] ) -> str :

 313 + """Build a focused corpus for one state bucket."""

 314 + blocks = []

 315 + for url in urls:

 316 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 317 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 318 + if not text:

 319 + continue

 320 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 321 +

 322 + # Add LinkedIn snippets for this state

 323 + state_snippets = [ls for ls in linkedin_snippets if ls.get( "state_hint" ) == state_code]

 324 + if state_snippets:

 325 + blocks.append( "\n===== LinkedIn snippets =====" )

 326 + for ls in state_snippets:

 327 + blocks.append(

 328 + f"\n-- [ {ls.get( 'state_hint' , '' )} ] {ls.get( 'title' , '' )} \n"

 329 + f"URL: {ls.get( 'url' , '' )} \n"

 330 + f" {ls.get( 'snippet' , '' )} \n"

 331 + )

 332 + return "" .join(blocks)

 334 +

 335 + def build_national_corpus ( urls: list [ str ] ) -> str :

 336 + """Build the national bucket corpus."""

 337 + blocks = []

 338 + for url in urls:

 339 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 340 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 341 + if not text:

 342 + continue

 343 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 344 + return "" .join(blocks)

 347 + def extract_per_state (

 348 + state_buckets: dict [ str , list [ str ]],

 349 + national_urls: list [ str ],

 350 + linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ],

 351 + ) -> list [ dict ]:

 352 + """Run Haiku once per state bucket + once for national. Merge results."""

 353 + all_items: list [ dict ] = []

 354 +

 355 + # Per-state calls

 356 + for state_code in sorted (state_buckets.keys()):

 357 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 358 + urls = state_buckets[state_code]

 359 + print ( f"\n === State bucket: {state_code} ( {state_name} ) — { len (urls)} URLs ===" )

 360 + corpus = build_state_corpus(state_code, urls, linkedin_snippets)

 361 + if not corpus.strip():

 362 + print ( f" (empty corpus, skipping)" )

 363 + continue

 364 + prompt = PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE. format (

 365 + state_name=state_name,

 366 + state_code=state_code,

 367 + corpus=corpus[: 500000 ],

 368 + )

 369 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label=state_code)

 370 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for {state_code} " )

 371 + all_items.extend(items)

 372 +

 373 + # National bucket

 374 + print ( f"\n === National bucket — { len (national_urls)} URLs ===" )

 375 + national_corpus = build_national_corpus(national_urls)

 376 + if national_corpus.strip():

 377 + prompt = NATIONAL_PROMPT + national_corpus[: 500000 ]

 378 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label= "national" )

 379 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for national" )

 380 + all_items.extend(items)

 381 +

 382 + return all_items

 386 + # Phase 4 — District resolver (for entries with empty district)

 387 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 390 + def resolve_district ( name: str , state_code: str ) -> tuple [ str , str ]:

 391 + """Reverse-lookup employer district for a CSTA chapter leader.

 392 + Returns (district_name, confidence) where confidence is high|medium|low|none.

 393 + Only called for territory-state entries with empty district. """

 394 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 395 + queries = [

 396 + f'" {name} " "computer science" teacher {state_name} ' ,

 397 + f'" {name} " CSTA {state_name} school district' ,

 398 + ]

 399 + all_snippets = []

 400 + for q in queries:

 401 + try :

 402 + orgs = serper_search(q, num= 5 )

 403 + for o in orgs[: 5 ]:

 404 + title = o.get( "title" , "" )

 405 + snippet = o.get( "snippet" , "" )

 406 + link = o.get( "link" , "" )

 407 + if snippet or title:

 408 + all_snippets.append( f"- [ {title[: 80 ]} ] {snippet[: 200 ]} (source: {link[: 80 ]} )" )

 409 + time.sleep( 0.4 )

 410 + except Exception as e:

 411 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Serper failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 412 +

 413 + if not all_snippets:

 414 + return "" , "none"

 415 +

 416 + snippets_text = "\n" .join(all_snippets[: 12 ])

 417 + prompt = f"""Based ONLY on these search results, what K-12 school district does {name} currently work at in {state_name} ?

 418 +

 419 + Rules:

 420 + - Return exactly: `district_name|confidence` where confidence is high, medium, low, or none.

 421 + - Return `|none` if no district is clearly named in the snippets.

 422 + - Do NOT guess. Do NOT fabricate. Prefer LinkedIn/school-directory hits.

 423 + - `district_name` must be a real K- 12 LEA or charter school.

 424 + - Example outputs:

 425 + Vallejo City Unified School District|high

 426 + Stoneham Public Schools|medium

 427 + |none

 428 +

 429 + Search results:

 430 + {snippets_text}

 431 +

 432 + Your answer (district_name|confidence): """

 433 +

 434 + try :

 435 + import anthropic

 436 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 60.0 )

 437 + resp = client.messages.create(

 438 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 439 + max_tokens= 150 ,

 440 + temperature= 0.0 ,

 441 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 442 + )

 443 + answer = resp.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 444 + # Strip code fences if any

 445 + answer = answer.replace( "`" , "" ).strip()

 446 + if "|" in answer:

 447 + district, confidence = answer.split( "|" , 1 )

 448 + return district.strip(), confidence.strip().lower()

 449 + except Exception as e:

 450 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Haiku failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 451 + return "" , "none"

 452 +

 453 +

 454 + def run_district_resolver ( entries: list [ dict ] ) -> tuple [ int , int ]:

 455 + """For each territory-state entry with empty district, try to resolve one.

 456 + Mutates entries in place. Returns (attempted, resolved). """

 457 + territory = TERRITORY_STATES_WITH_CA

 458 + attempted = 0

 459 + resolved = 0

 460 + for e in entries:

 461 + state = (e.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 462 + district = (e.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 463 + name = (e.get( "name" ) or "" ).strip()

 464 + if district:

 465 + continue

 466 + if state not in territory:

 467 + continue

 468 + if not name:

 469 + continue

 470 + attempted += 1

 471 + print ( f" resolving {name} ( {state} )..." )

 472 + d, conf = resolve_district(name, state)

 473 + if d and conf in ( "high" , "medium" ):

 474 + e[ "district" ] = d

 475 + e[ "district_resolver_confidence" ] = conf

 476 + resolved += 1

 477 + print ( f" ✓ {d} ( {conf} )" )

 478 + elif d:

 479 + print ( f" ~ {d} ( {conf} ) — low confidence, skipped" )

 480 + return attempted, resolved

 481 +

 482 +

 … +92 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿  (timeout 3m)

 ⏺ Root cause isolated. Haiku is NOT silently dropping entries — it correctly extracts 14 entries (5 MI, 5 NE, 4 MA) from a focused 22K-char corpus containing only state chapter home pages. But in production,

 these same entries get lost when Haiku processes the full 610K-char corpus (national pages + all chapters + LinkedIn snippets).

 Saturation problem: Large mixed corpus drowns out small state chapter sections. Haiku prioritizes the louder national content and produces only ~33 entries total.

 Change 1 — Per-state Haiku extraction (fixes silent drop). Group fetched URLs by state prefix ( michigan.csteachers.org → MI bucket), plus a "national" bucket for csteachers.org/* URLs. Run Haiku once per

 bucket. Merge results. Cost: ~13 small calls instead of 1 big call, total ~$0.30 (still cheap).

 Expected yield: 33 → 60-80 total entries, 16 → 35-45 matchable (once we also pick up districts where Haiku can find them).

 Change 2 — District resolver pass (boosts matchable). For entries with district="" , run a Serper query like "{name}" computer science teacher {state_name} and feed top snippets to Haiku: "What K-12 district

 does this person work at?" Update only on confident answer. This handles the "chapter page lists name but not employer" problem that affects every state except CA.

 Expected yield from resolver: ~50% hit rate on the ~45 non-matchable entries → +20-22 additional districts. Final roster: 50-60 matchable (vs today's 16).

 Approve and I'll execute both changes in one commit, then rerun end-to-end and report final counts. Or we can do just Change 1 first if you want to see the cheaper win before adding the resolver.

 ❯ /effort high

 ⎿ Set effort level to high: Comprehensive implementation with extensive testing and documentation

 ❯ approved

 ⏺ Executing. Rewriting the fetcher with per-state bucketing + district resolver pass.

 1 #!/usr/bin/env python3

 2 - """Build memory/csta_roster.json — one-shot CSTA chapter leader roster .

 2 + """Build memory/csta_roster.json — CSTA chapter leader roster with per-state

 3 + extraction + district resolver pass .

 4 - Two-phase pipeline:

 5 - Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on

 6 - csteachers.org subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 7 - Phase 2 (Extraction): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL

 8 - with a browser User-Agent, combine text with national seed pages and

 9 - LinkedIn snippets, feed to Claude Haiku for structured extraction.

 5 + Session 58 rewrite — fixes the saturation problem where a single Haiku call

 6 + on the full 610K-char corpus silently drops state chapter members in favor

 7 + of the louder national CSTA content. Splits extraction by state bucket.

 8 + Then resolves missing K- 12 employer districts via Serper + Haiku reverse

 9 + lookup for territory-state entries.

 11 - Why this shape: CSTA rosters are essentially static directory data. Daily

 12 - scanning is architecturally wrong (see project_f5_csta_scanner_low_yield.md

 13 - and BUG 2 plan). Fetch once, cache to JSON, enrich F2 (and future scanners)

 14 - inline via signal_processor.enrich_with_csta.

 11 + Pipeline:

 12 + Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on csteachers.org

 13 + subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 14 + Phase 2 (Fetch): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL with a

 15 + browser User-Agent. Bucket URLs by state subdomain prefix.

 16 + Phase 3 (Per-state extraction): Haiku extraction, one call per state bucket

 17 + plus one call for the national bucket. Per-state prompts inject the state

 18 + code so Haiku correctly tags employer state by default.

 19 + Phase 4 (District resolver): For territory-state entries still missing a

 20 + district, run Serper ` "{name}" "computer science" teacher {state_name}`

 21 + and feed snippets to Haiku for a reverse-lookup employer extraction.

 22 + Phase 5 (Dedup + write).

 24 Run: python3 scripts/fetch_csta_roster.py

 25 Output: memory/csta_roster.json

 18 - Cost: ~$0. 10 one-time (Serper + Haiku ) .

 26 + Cost: ~$ 0. 40 one-time (Serper + Haiku , up from ~$ 0 . 10 for the v1 single-call

 27 + version).

 28 Refresh: manually, quarterly or when CSTA announces new chapter boards.

 215 + def bucket_urls_by_state ( urls: set [ str ] ) -> tuple [ dict [ str , list [ str ]], list [ str ]]:

 312 + def build_state_corpus ( state_code: str , urls: list [ str ], linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ] ) -> str :

 313 + """Build a focused corpus for one state bucket."""

 314 + blocks = []

 315 + for url in urls:

 316 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 317 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 318 + if not text:

 319 + continue

 320 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 321 +

 322 + # Add LinkedIn snippets for this state

 323 + state_snippets = [ls for ls in linkedin_snippets if ls.get( "state_hint" ) == state_code]

 324 + if state_snippets:

 325 + blocks.append( "\n===== LinkedIn snippets =====" )

 326 + for ls in state_snippets:

 327 + blocks.append(

 328 + f"\n-- [ {ls.get( 'state_hint' , '' )} ] {ls.get( 'title' , '' )} \n"

 329 + f"URL: {ls.get( 'url' , '' )} \n"

 330 + f" {ls.get( 'snippet' , '' )} \n"

 331 + )

 332 + return "" .join(blocks)

 333 +

 334 +

 335 + def build_national_corpus ( urls: list [ str ] ) -> str :

 336 + """Build the national bucket corpus."""

 337 + blocks = []

 338 + for url in urls:

 339 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 340 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 341 + if not text:

 342 + continue

 343 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 344 + return "" .join(blocks)

 346 +

 347 + def extract_per_state (

 348 + state_buckets: dict [ str , list [ str ]],

 349 + national_urls: list [ str ],

 350 + linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ],

 351 + ) -> list [ dict ]:

 352 + """Run Haiku once per state bucket + once for national. Merge results."""

 353 + all_items: list [ dict ] = []

 354 +

 355 + # Per-state calls

 356 + for state_code in sorted (state_buckets.keys()):

 357 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 358 + urls = state_buckets[state_code]

 359 + print ( f"\n === State bucket: {state_code} ( {state_name} ) — { len (urls)} URLs ===" )

 360 + corpus = build_state_corpus(state_code, urls, linkedin_snippets)

 361 + if not corpus.strip():

 362 + print ( f" (empty corpus, skipping)" )

 363 + continue

 364 + prompt = PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE. format (

 365 + state_name=state_name,

 366 + state_code=state_code,

 367 + corpus=corpus[: 500000 ],

 368 + )

 369 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label=state_code)

 370 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for {state_code} " )

 371 + all_items.extend(items)

 372 +

 373 + # National bucket

 374 + print ( f"\n === National bucket — { len (national_urls)} URLs ===" )

 375 + national_corpus = build_national_corpus(national_urls)

 376 + if national_corpus.strip():

 377 + prompt = NATIONAL_PROMPT + national_corpus[: 500000 ]

 378 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label= "national" )

 379 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for national" )

 380 + all_items.extend(items)

 381 +

 382 + return all_items

 383 +

 233 - # Merge + dedup + write

 386 + # Phase 4 — District resolver (for entries with empty district)

 387 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 390 + def resolve_district ( name: str , state_code: str ) -> tuple [ str , str ]:

 391 + """Reverse-lookup employer district for a CSTA chapter leader.

 392 + Returns (district_name, confidence) where confidence is high|medium|low|none.

 393 + Only called for territory-state entries with empty district. """

 394 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 395 + queries = [

 396 + f'" {name} " "computer science" teacher {state_name} ' ,

 397 + f'" {name} " CSTA {state_name} school district' ,

 398 + ]

 399 + all_snippets = []

 400 + for q in queries:

 401 + try :

 402 + orgs = serper_search(q, num= 5 )

 403 + for o in orgs[: 5 ]:

 404 + title = o.get( "title" , "" )

 405 + snippet = o.get( "snippet" , "" )

 406 + link = o.get( "link" , "" )

 407 + if snippet or title:

 408 + all_snippets.append( f"- [ {title[: 80 ]} ] {snippet[: 200 ]} (source: {link[: 80 ]} )" )

 409 + time.sleep( 0.4 )

 410 + except Exception as e:

 411 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Serper failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 412 +

 413 + if not all_snippets:

 414 + return "" , "none"

 415 +

 416 + snippets_text = "\n" .join(all_snippets[: 12 ])

 417 + prompt = f"""Based ONLY on these search results, what K-12 school district does {name} currently work at in {state_name} ?

 418 +

 419 + Rules:

 420 + - Return exactly: `district_name|confidence` where confidence is high, medium, low, or none.

 421 + - Return `|none` if no district is clearly named in the snippets.

 422 + - Do NOT guess. Do NOT fabricate. Prefer LinkedIn/school-directory hits.

 423 + - `district_name` must be a real K- 12 LEA or charter school.

 424 + - Example outputs:

 425 + Vallejo City Unified School District|high

 426 + Stoneham Public Schools|medium

 427 + |none

 428 +

 429 + Search results:

 430 + {snippets_text}

 431 +

 432 + Your answer (district_name|confidence): """

 433 +

 434 + try :

 435 + import anthropic

 436 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 60.0 )

 437 + resp = client.messages.create(

 438 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 439 + max_tokens= 150 ,

 440 + temperature= 0.0 ,

 441 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 442 + )

 443 + answer = resp.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 444 + # Strip code fences if any

 445 + answer = answer.replace( "`" , "" ).strip()

 446 + if "|" in answer:

 447 + district, confidence = answer.split( "|" , 1 )

 448 + return district.strip(), confidence.strip().lower()

 449 + except Exception as e:

 450 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Haiku failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 451 + return "" , "none"

 452 +

 453 +

 454 + def run_district_resolver ( entries: list [ dict ] ) -> tuple [ int , int ]:

 455 + """For each territory-state entry with empty district, try to resolve one.

 456 + Mutates entries in place. Returns (attempted, resolved). """

 457 + territory = TERRITORY_STATES_WITH_CA

 458 + attempted = 0

 459 + resolved = 0

 460 + for e in entries:

 461 + state = (e.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 462 + district = (e.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 463 + name = (e.get( "name" ) or "" ).strip()

 464 + if district:

 465 + continue

 466 + if state not in territory:

 467 + continue

 468 + if not name:

 469 + continue

 470 + attempted += 1

 471 + print ( f" resolving {name} ( {state} )..." )

 472 + d, conf = resolve_district(name, state)

 473 + if d and conf in ( "high" , "medium" ):

 474 + e[ "district" ] = d

 475 + e[ "district_resolver_confidence" ] = conf

 476 + resolved += 1

 477 + print ( f" ✓ {d} ( {conf} )" )

 478 + elif d:

 479 + print ( f" ~ {d} ( {conf} ) — low confidence, skipped" )

 480 + return attempted, resolved

 481 +

 482 +

 ⏺ Two regressions surfaced:

 1. CA dropped from 12 → 0 entries. Because CA uses regional sub-chapter subdomains ( goldengate.csteachers.org , greaterlosangeles.csteachers.org , siliconvalley.csteachers.org ) that don't match my state-name

 regex. They went to national bucket, which returned 0.

 2. National bucket returned 0 items despite having 60+ URLs including the CSTA team/board/article pages. The strict national prompt over-filtered.

 But the resolver is working beautifully — resolved 12 of 16 attempts with real district names (Troy Public Schools, Minden Public Schools, Somerville Public Schools, etc.). Fixing the regressions now.

 ⏺ Key finding: Haiku fetches michigan.csteachers.org and nebraska.csteachers.org, but extracts ZERO entries from them despite the pages clearly listing boards (Josh Pudaloff - Chapter President, Tiffiny

 Widdifield - President). Before proposing a fix, let me do one more empirical probe: run Haiku on a MI-only corpus and capture raw output to see exactly what it's returning.

 ⏺ Bash (source .venv/bin/activate && python3 << 'EOF'

 import os, json, httpx…)

 ⎿   fetched https://michigan.csteachers.org/ (2868 chars)

 fetched https://nebraska.csteachers.org/ (2467 chars)

 fetched https://illinois.csteachers.org/ (4819 chars)

 … +92 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿  (timeout 3m)

 ⏺ Root cause isolated. Haiku is NOT silently dropping entries — it correctly extracts 14 entries (5 MI, 5 NE, 4 MA) from a focused 22K-char corpus containing only state chapter home pages. But in production,

 these same entries get lost when Haiku processes the full 610K-char corpus (national pages + all chapters + LinkedIn snippets).

 Saturation problem: Large mixed corpus drowns out small state chapter sections. Haiku prioritizes the louder national content and produces only ~33 entries total.

 Change 1 — Per-state Haiku extraction (fixes silent drop). Group fetched URLs by state prefix ( michigan.csteachers.org → MI bucket), plus a "national" bucket for csteachers.org/* URLs. Run Haiku once per

 bucket. Merge results. Cost: ~13 small calls instead of 1 big call, total ~$0.30 (still cheap).

 Expected yield: 33 → 60-80 total entries, 16 → 35-45 matchable (once we also pick up districts where Haiku can find them).

 Change 2 — District resolver pass (boosts matchable). For entries with district="" , run a Serper query like "{name}" computer science teacher {state_name} and feed top snippets to Haiku: "What K-12 district

 does this person work at?" Update only on confident answer. This handles the "chapter page lists name but not employer" problem that affects every state except CA.

 Expected yield from resolver: ~50% hit rate on the ~45 non-matchable entries → +20-22 additional districts. Final roster: 50-60 matchable (vs today's 16).

 Approve and I'll execute both changes in one commit, then rerun end-to-end and report final counts. Or we can do just Change 1 first if you want to see the cheaper win before adding the resolver.

 ❯ /effort high

 ⎿ Set effort level to high: Comprehensive implementation with extensive testing and documentation

 ❯ approved

 ⏺ Executing. Rewriting the fetcher with per-state bucketing + district resolver pass.

 1 #!/usr/bin/env python3

 2 - """Build memory/csta_roster.json — one-shot CSTA chapter leader roster .

 2 + """Build memory/csta_roster.json — CSTA chapter leader roster with per-state

 3 + extraction + district resolver pass .

 4 - Two-phase pipeline:

 5 - Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on

 6 - csteachers.org subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 7 - Phase 2 (Extraction): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL

 8 - with a browser User-Agent, combine text with national seed pages and

 9 - LinkedIn snippets, feed to Claude Haiku for structured extraction.

 5 + Session 58 rewrite — fixes the saturation problem where a single Haiku call

 6 + on the full 610K-char corpus silently drops state chapter members in favor

 7 + of the louder national CSTA content. Splits extraction by state bucket.

 8 + Then resolves missing K- 12 employer districts via Serper + Haiku reverse

 9 + lookup for territory-state entries.

 11 - Why this shape: CSTA rosters are essentially static directory data. Daily

 12 - scanning is architecturally wrong (see project_f5_csta_scanner_low_yield.md

 13 - and BUG 2 plan). Fetch once, cache to JSON, enrich F2 (and future scanners)

 14 - inline via signal_processor.enrich_with_csta.

 11 + Pipeline:

 12 + Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on csteachers.org

 13 + subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 14 + Phase 2 (Fetch): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL with a

 15 + browser User-Agent. Bucket URLs by state subdomain prefix.

 16 + Phase 3 (Per-state extraction): Haiku extraction, one call per state bucket

 17 + plus one call for the national bucket. Per-state prompts inject the state

 18 + code so Haiku correctly tags employer state by default.

 19 + Phase 4 (District resolver): For territory-state entries still missing a

 20 + district, run Serper ` "{name}" "computer science" teacher {state_name}`

 21 + and feed snippets to Haiku for a reverse-lookup employer extraction.

 22 + Phase 5 (Dedup + write).

 24 Run: python3 scripts/fetch_csta_roster.py

 25 Output: memory/csta_roster.json

 18 - Cost: ~$0. 10 one-time (Serper + Haiku ) .

 26 + Cost: ~$ 0. 40 one-time (Serper + Haiku , up from ~$ 0 . 10 for the v1 single-call

 27 + version).

 28 Refresh: manually, quarterly or when CSTA announces new chapter boards.

 196 + def url_to_state_code ( url: str ) -> str :

 197 + """Extract state code from URL subdomain pattern like 'https://michigan.csteachers.org/...'.

 198 + Returns '' if URL doesn 't match a state subdomain (e.g. bare csteachers.org)."""

 199 + m = re.match( r"https?://([a-z]+)\.csteachers\.org" , url.lower())

 200 + if not m:

 201 + # Also check community subdomain patterns: community.csteachers.org/csta{state}/home

 202 + m2 = re.search( r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+?)silverstate" , url.lower())

 203 + if m2:

 204 + sub = m2.group( 1 )

 205 + return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "" )

 206 + m3 = re.search( r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+)/" , url.lower())

 207 + if m3:

 208 + sub = m3.group( 1 )

 209 + return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "" )

 215 + def bucket_urls_by_state ( urls: set [ str ] ) -> tuple [ dict [ str , list [ str ]], list [ str ]]:

 216 + """Partition URLs into state buckets + a national bucket.

 217 + Returns (state_buckets, national_list). """

 218 + state_buckets: dict [ str , list [ str ]] = {}

 219 + national: list [ str ] = []

 220 + for u in sorted (urls):

 221 + code = url_to_state_code(u)

 222 + if code:

 223 + state_buckets.setdefault(code, []).append(u)

 224 + else :

 225 + national.append(u)

 226 + return state_buckets, national

 234 + PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract CSTA (Computer Science Teachers Association) chapter board members and officers from these pages.

 235 +

 236 + CONTEXT: These pages are specifically for the CSTA {state_name} chapter (state code: {state_code}). Default `state` to "{state_code}" for every person unless the page EXPLICITLY says the person

 + teaches in a different state.

 237 +

 238 For each person, return JSON:

 239 {{

 240 "name" : "Full name" ,

 177 - "role": "Chapter role — e.g. 'President', 'Vice President', 'Secretary', 'K-12 Board Member', 'Treasurer'",

 178 - "chapter": "CSTA chapter name — e.g. 'CSTA Pittsburgh ', 'CSTA Longwood ' , 'CSTA Arizona' ",

 179 - "state": "2-letter state code of their K-12 SCHOOL EMPLOYER, NOT their chapter's state (usually the same, but not always)",

 241 + "role" : "Chapter role — e.g. 'President', 'Vice President', 'Secretary', 'K-12 Board Member', 'Treasurer' , 'Chapter Leader' " ,

 242 + "chapter" : "CSTA chapter name — e.g. 'CSTA {state_name} ', 'CSTA Pittsburgh '" ,

 243 + "state" : "2-letter state code — MUST default to '{state_code}' unless page says otherwise" ,

 244 "district" : "Exact K-12 school district name where this person works. Empty string '' if the page does not explicitly name a district — do NOT guess, do NOT use the chapter city as a district

 , do NOT fabricate." ,

 245 "source_url" : "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input that mentions this person. Do not fabricate."

 246 }}

 248 STRICT RULES:

 185 - - Only STATE or REGIONAL chapter officers/board members. SKIP the national

 186 - CSTA Board of Directors (they are too disconnected from any single district).

 249 - SKIP university professors and college advisors — K- 12 only.

 188 - - SKIP general CSTA staff / HQ employees unless they also hold a state /regional

 189 - chapter role.

 250 + - SKIP general CSTA national HQ staff unless they ALSO hold this state chapter role.

 251 - SKIP conference speakers unless explicitly listed as chapter officers.

 191 - - `state` is the employer state. A person on the CSTA Chicago chapter whose

 192 - teaching job is in Gary, Indiana gets state="IN".

 193 - - `district` must be a real named K-12 LEA. If the page says "high school

 194 - computer science teacher" without naming the district, return `district: ""`.

 195 - - `source_url` must be exactly one of the URL lines shown in the input.

 252 + - `state` MUST be a 2 -letter uppercase code. Default to "{state_code}" .

 253 + - `district` must be a named K- 12 LEA. Return "" if the page just says "computer science teacher" without naming a district.

 254 + - Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Empty array [] if nothing qualifies.

 197 - Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Empty array [] if nothing qualifies.

 198 -

 256 Pages:

 257 {corpus} """

 265 + "role" : "Chapter role" ,

 269 + "source_url" : "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input."

 271 +

 272 + STRICT RULES:

 274 + - SKIP university professors and higher-ed.

 275 + - SKIP national CSTA HQ staff unless they ALSO hold a state/regional chapter role.

 276 + - `state` MUST be a 2 -letter uppercase code. Do not return full state names.

 277 + - `district` must be a named K- 12 LEA. Return "" if not explicit.

 278 + - Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Empty array [] if nothing qualifies.

 279 +

 280 + Pages:

 281 + """

 312 + def build_state_corpus ( state_code: str , urls: list [ str ], linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ] ) -> str :

 313 + """Build a focused corpus for one state bucket."""

 314 + blocks = []

 315 + for url in urls:

 316 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 317 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 318 + if not text:

 319 + continue

 320 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 321 +

 322 + # Add LinkedIn snippets for this state

 323 + state_snippets = [ls for ls in linkedin_snippets if ls.get( "state_hint" ) == state_code]

 324 + if state_snippets:

 325 + blocks.append( "\n===== LinkedIn snippets =====" )

 326 + for ls in state_snippets:

 327 + blocks.append(

 328 + f"\n-- [ {ls.get( 'state_hint' , '' )} ] {ls.get( 'title' , '' )} \n"

 329 + f"URL: {ls.get( 'url' , '' )} \n"

 330 + f" {ls.get( 'snippet' , '' )} \n"

 331 + )

 332 + return "" .join(blocks)

 333 +

 334 +

 335 + def build_national_corpus ( urls: list [ str ] ) -> str :

 336 + """Build the national bucket corpus."""

 337 + blocks = []

 338 + for url in urls:

 339 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 340 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 341 + if not text:

 342 + continue

 343 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 344 + return "" .join(blocks)

 346 +

 347 + def extract_per_state (

 348 + state_buckets: dict [ str , list [ str ]],

 349 + national_urls: list [ str ],

 350 + linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ],

 351 + ) -> list [ dict ]:

 352 + """Run Haiku once per state bucket + once for national. Merge results."""

 353 + all_items: list [ dict ] = []

 354 +

 355 + # Per-state calls

 356 + for state_code in sorted (state_buckets.keys()):

 357 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 358 + urls = state_buckets[state_code]

 359 + print ( f"\n === State bucket: {state_code} ( {state_name} ) — { len (urls)} URLs ===" )

 360 + corpus = build_state_corpus(state_code, urls, linkedin_snippets)

 361 + if not corpus.strip():

 362 + print ( f" (empty corpus, skipping)" )

 363 + continue

 364 + prompt = PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE. format (

 365 + state_name=state_name,

 366 + state_code=state_code,

 367 + corpus=corpus[: 500000 ],

 368 + )

 369 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label=state_code)

 370 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for {state_code} " )

 371 + all_items.extend(items)

 372 +

 373 + # National bucket

 374 + print ( f"\n === National bucket — { len (national_urls)} URLs ===" )

 375 + national_corpus = build_national_corpus(national_urls)

 376 + if national_corpus.strip():

 377 + prompt = NATIONAL_PROMPT + national_corpus[: 500000 ]

 378 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label= "national" )

 379 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for national" )

 380 + all_items.extend(items)

 381 +

 382 + return all_items

 383 +

 384 +

 385 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 233 - # Merge + dedup + write

 386 + # Phase 4 — District resolver (for entries with empty district)

 387 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 390 + def resolve_district ( name: str , state_code: str ) -> tuple [ str , str ]:

 391 + """Reverse-lookup employer district for a CSTA chapter leader.

 392 + Returns (district_name, confidence) where confidence is high|medium|low|none.

 393 + Only called for territory-state entries with empty district. """

 394 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 395 + queries = [

 396 + f'" {name} " "computer science" teacher {state_name} ' ,

 397 + f'" {name} " CSTA {state_name} school district' ,

 398 + ]

 399 + all_snippets = []

 400 + for q in queries:

 401 + try :

 402 + orgs = serper_search(q, num= 5 )

 403 + for o in orgs[: 5 ]:

 404 + title = o.get( "title" , "" )

 405 + snippet = o.get( "snippet" , "" )

 406 + link = o.get( "link" , "" )

 407 + if snippet or title:

 408 + all_snippets.append( f"- [ {title[: 80 ]} ] {snippet[: 200 ]} (source: {link[: 80 ]} )" )

 409 + time.sleep( 0.4 )

 410 + except Exception as e:

 411 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Serper failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 412 +

 413 + if not all_snippets:

 414 + return "" , "none"

 415 +

 416 + snippets_text = "\n" .join(all_snippets[: 12 ])

 417 + prompt = f"""Based ONLY on these search results, what K-12 school district does {name} currently work at in {state_name} ?

 418 +

 419 + Rules:

 420 + - Return exactly: `district_name|confidence` where confidence is high, medium, low, or none.

 421 + - Return `|none` if no district is clearly named in the snippets.

 422 + - Do NOT guess. Do NOT fabricate. Prefer LinkedIn/school-directory hits.

 423 + - `district_name` must be a real K- 12 LEA or charter school.

 424 + - Example outputs:

 425 + Vallejo City Unified School District|high

 426 + Stoneham Public Schools|medium

 427 + |none

 428 +

 429 + Search results:

 430 + {snippets_text}

 431 +

 432 + Your answer (district_name|confidence): """

 433 +

 434 + try :

 435 + import anthropic

 436 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 60.0 )

 437 + resp = client.messages.create(

 438 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 439 + max_tokens= 150 ,

 440 + temperature= 0.0 ,

 441 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 442 + )

 443 + answer = resp.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 444 + # Strip code fences if any

 445 + answer = answer.replace( "`" , "" ).strip()

 446 + if "|" in answer:

 447 + district, confidence = answer.split( "|" , 1 )

 448 + return district.strip(), confidence.strip().lower()

 449 + except Exception as e:

 450 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Haiku failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 451 + return "" , "none"

 452 +

 453 +

 454 + def run_district_resolver ( entries: list [ dict ] ) -> tuple [ int , int ]:

 455 + """For each territory-state entry with empty district, try to resolve one.

 456 + Mutates entries in place. Returns (attempted, resolved). """

 457 + territory = TERRITORY_STATES_WITH_CA

 458 + attempted = 0

 459 + resolved = 0

 460 + for e in entries:

 461 + state = (e.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 462 + district = (e.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 463 + name = (e.get( "name" ) or "" ).strip()

 464 + if district:

 465 + continue

 466 + if state not in territory:

 467 + continue

 468 + if not name:

 469 + continue

 470 + attempted += 1

 471 + print ( f" resolving {name} ( {state} )..." )

 472 + d, conf = resolve_district(name, state)

 473 + if d and conf in ( "high" , "medium" ):

 474 + e[ "district" ] = d

 475 + e[ "district_resolver_confidence" ] = conf

 476 + resolved += 1

 477 + print ( f" ✓ {d} ( {conf} )" )

 478 + elif d:

 479 + print ( f" ~ {d} ( {conf} ) — low confidence, skipped" )

 480 + return attempted, resolved

 481 +

 482 +

 483 + # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 484 + # Phase 5 — Dedup + write

 485 + # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 486 +

 ⏺ Two regressions surfaced:

 1. CA dropped from 12 → 0 entries. Because CA uses regional sub-chapter subdomains ( goldengate.csteachers.org , greaterlosangeles.csteachers.org , siliconvalley.csteachers.org ) that don't match my state-name

 regex. They went to national bucket, which returned 0.

 2. National bucket returned 0 items despite having 60+ URLs including the CSTA team/board/article pages. The strict national prompt over-filtered.

 But the resolver is working beautifully — resolved 12 of 16 attempts with real district names (Troy Public Schools, Minden Public Schools, Somerville Public Schools, etc.). Fixing the regressions now.

 262 + def is_national_signal_url ( url: str ) -> bool :

 263 + """Return True if the URL should survive into the national bucket.

 264 + Filters out noise (PD articles, event calendars, jobs, schoolsofed, etc.). """

 265 + # Skip jobs subdomain, conference subdomain, landscape subdomain

 266 + lower = url.lower()

 267 + if any (x in lower for x in ( "jobs.csteachers.org" , "conference.csteachers.org" ,

 268 + "landscape.csteachers.org" , "my.csteachers.org" )):

 269 + return False

 270 + # Keep if any whitelist pattern matches

 271 + return any (p in url for p in NATIONAL_URL_WHITELIST_PATTERNS)

 Widdifield - President). Before proposing a fix, let me do one more empirical probe: run Haiku on a MI-only corpus and capture raw output to see exactly what it's returning.

 import os, json, httpx…)

 … +92 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿  (timeout 3m)

 ⏺ Root cause isolated. Haiku is NOT silently dropping entries — it correctly extracts 14 entries (5 MI, 5 NE, 4 MA) from a focused 22K-char corpus containing only state chapter home pages. But in production,

 these same entries get lost when Haiku processes the full 610K-char corpus (national pages + all chapters + LinkedIn snippets).

 Saturation problem: Large mixed corpus drowns out small state chapter sections. Haiku prioritizes the louder national content and produces only ~33 entries total.

 Change 1 — Per-state Haiku extraction (fixes silent drop). Group fetched URLs by state prefix ( michigan.csteachers.org → MI bucket), plus a "national" bucket for csteachers.org/* URLs. Run Haiku once per

 bucket. Merge results. Cost: ~13 small calls instead of 1 big call, total ~$0.30 (still cheap).

 Expected yield: 33 → 60-80 total entries, 16 → 35-45 matchable (once we also pick up districts where Haiku can find them).

 Change 2 — District resolver pass (boosts matchable). For entries with district="" , run a Serper query like "{name}" computer science teacher {state_name} and feed top snippets to Haiku: "What K-12 district

 does this person work at?" Update only on confident answer. This handles the "chapter page lists name but not employer" problem that affects every state except CA.

 Expected yield from resolver: ~50% hit rate on the ~45 non-matchable entries → +20-22 additional districts. Final roster: 50-60 matchable (vs today's 16).

 Approve and I'll execute both changes in one commit, then rerun end-to-end and report final counts. Or we can do just Change 1 first if you want to see the cheaper win before adding the resolver.

 ❯ /effort high

 ⎿ Set effort level to high: Comprehensive implementation with extensive testing and documentation

 ❯ approved

 ⏺ Executing. Rewriting the fetcher with per-state bucketing + district resolver pass.

 1 #!/usr/bin/env python3

 2 - """Build memory/csta_roster.json — one-shot CSTA chapter leader roster .

 2 + """Build memory/csta_roster.json — CSTA chapter leader roster with per-state

 3 + extraction + district resolver pass .

 4 - Two-phase pipeline:

 5 - Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on

 6 - csteachers.org subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 7 - Phase 2 (Extraction): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL

 8 - with a browser User-Agent, combine text with national seed pages and

 9 - LinkedIn snippets, feed to Claude Haiku for structured extraction.

 5 + Session 58 rewrite — fixes the saturation problem where a single Haiku call

 6 + on the full 610K-char corpus silently drops state chapter members in favor

 7 + of the louder national CSTA content. Splits extraction by state bucket.

 8 + Then resolves missing K- 12 employer districts via Serper + Haiku reverse

 9 + lookup for territory-state entries.

 11 - Why this shape: CSTA rosters are essentially static directory data. Daily

 12 - scanning is architecturally wrong (see project_f5_csta_scanner_low_yield.md

 13 - and BUG 2 plan). Fetch once, cache to JSON, enrich F2 (and future scanners)

 14 - inline via signal_processor.enrich_with_csta.

 11 + Pipeline:

 12 + Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on csteachers.org

 13 + subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 14 + Phase 2 (Fetch): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL with a

 15 + browser User-Agent. Bucket URLs by state subdomain prefix.

 16 + Phase 3 (Per-state extraction): Haiku extraction, one call per state bucket

 17 + plus one call for the national bucket. Per-state prompts inject the state

 18 + code so Haiku correctly tags employer state by default.

 19 + Phase 4 (District resolver): For territory-state entries still missing a

 20 + district, run Serper ` "{name}" "computer science" teacher {state_name}`

 21 + and feed snippets to Haiku for a reverse-lookup employer extraction.

 22 + Phase 5 (Dedup + write).

 24 Run: python3 scripts/fetch_csta_roster.py

 25 Output: memory/csta_roster.json

 18 - Cost: ~$0. 10 one-time (Serper + Haiku ) .

 26 + Cost: ~$ 0. 40 one-time (Serper + Haiku , up from ~$ 0 . 10 for the v1 single-call

 27 + version).

 28 Refresh: manually, quarterly or when CSTA announces new chapter boards.

 29 """

 172 - HAIKU_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract CSTA (Computer Science Teachers Association) state and regional CHAPTER board members and officers from these pages.

 234 + PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract CSTA (Computer Science Teachers Association) chapter board members and officers from these pages.

 235 +

 236 + CONTEXT: These pages are specifically for the CSTA {state_name} chapter (state code: {state_code}). Default `state` to "{state_code}" for every person unless the page EXPLICITLY says the person

 + teaches in a different state.

 237 +

 238 For each person, return JSON:

 239 {{

 240 "name" : "Full name" ,

 177 - "role": "Chapter role — e.g. 'President', 'Vice President', 'Secretary', 'K-12 Board Member', 'Treasurer'",

 178 - "chapter": "CSTA chapter name — e.g. 'CSTA Pittsburgh ', 'CSTA Longwood ' , 'CSTA Arizona' ",

 179 - "state": "2-letter state code of their K-12 SCHOOL EMPLOYER, NOT their chapter's state (usually the same, but not always)",

 241 + "role" : "Chapter role — e.g. 'President', 'Vice President', 'Secretary', 'K-12 Board Member', 'Treasurer' , 'Chapter Leader' " ,

 242 + "chapter" : "CSTA chapter name — e.g. 'CSTA {state_name} ', 'CSTA Pittsburgh '" ,

 243 + "state" : "2-letter state code — MUST default to '{state_code}' unless page says otherwise" ,

 244 "district" : "Exact K-12 school district name where this person works. Empty string '' if the page does not explicitly name a district — do NOT guess, do NOT use the chapter city as a district

 , do NOT fabricate." ,

 245 "source_url" : "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input that mentions this person. Do not fabricate."

 246 }}

 248 STRICT RULES:

 185 - - Only STATE or REGIONAL chapter officers/board members. SKIP the national

 186 - CSTA Board of Directors (they are too disconnected from any single district).

 249 - SKIP university professors and college advisors — K- 12 only.

 188 - - SKIP general CSTA staff / HQ employees unless they also hold a state /regional

 189 - chapter role.

 250 + - SKIP general CSTA national HQ staff unless they ALSO hold this state chapter role.

 251 - SKIP conference speakers unless explicitly listed as chapter officers.

 191 - - `state` is the employer state. A person on the CSTA Chicago chapter whose

 192 - teaching job is in Gary, Indiana gets state="IN".

 193 - - `district` must be a real named K-12 LEA. If the page says "high school

 194 - computer science teacher" without naming the district, return `district: ""`.

 312 + def build_state_corpus ( state_code: str , urls: list [ str ], linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ] ) -> str :

 313 + """Build a focused corpus for one state bucket."""

 314 + blocks = []

 315 + for url in urls:

 316 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 317 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 318 + if not text:

 319 + continue

 320 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 321 +

 322 + # Add LinkedIn snippets for this state

 323 + state_snippets = [ls for ls in linkedin_snippets if ls.get( "state_hint" ) == state_code]

 324 + if state_snippets:

 325 + blocks.append( "\n===== LinkedIn snippets =====" )

 326 + for ls in state_snippets:

 327 + blocks.append(

 328 + f"\n-- [ {ls.get( 'state_hint' , '' )} ] {ls.get( 'title' , '' )} \n"

 329 + f"URL: {ls.get( 'url' , '' )} \n"

 330 + f" {ls.get( 'snippet' , '' )} \n"

 331 + )

 332 + return "" .join(blocks)

 335 + def build_national_corpus ( urls: list [ str ] ) -> str :

 336 + """Build the national bucket corpus."""

 338 + for url in urls:

 347 + def extract_per_state (

 348 + state_buckets: dict [ str , list [ str ]],

 349 + national_urls: list [ str ],

 350 + linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ],

 351 + ) -> list [ dict ]:

 352 + """Run Haiku once per state bucket + once for national. Merge results."""

 353 + all_items: list [ dict ] = []

 354 +

 355 + # Per-state calls

 356 + for state_code in sorted (state_buckets.keys()):

 357 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 358 + urls = state_buckets[state_code]

 359 + print ( f"\n === State bucket: {state_code} ( {state_name} ) — { len (urls)} URLs ===" )

 360 + corpus = build_state_corpus(state_code, urls, linkedin_snippets)

 361 + if not corpus.strip():

 362 + print ( f" (empty corpus, skipping)" )

 363 + continue

 364 + prompt = PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE. format (

 365 + state_name=state_name,

 366 + state_code=state_code,

 367 + corpus=corpus[: 500000 ],

 368 + )

 369 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label=state_code)

 370 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for {state_code} " )

 371 + all_items.extend(items)

 372 +

 373 + # National bucket

 374 + print ( f"\n === National bucket — { len (national_urls)} URLs ===" )

 375 + national_corpus = build_national_corpus(national_urls)

 376 + if national_corpus.strip():

 377 + prompt = NATIONAL_PROMPT + national_corpus[: 500000 ]

 378 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label= "national" )

 379 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for national" )

 380 + all_items.extend(items)

 381 +

 382 + return all_items

 386 + # Phase 4 — District resolver (for entries with empty district)

 387 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 390 + def resolve_district ( name: str , state_code: str ) -> tuple [ str , str ]:

 391 + """Reverse-lookup employer district for a CSTA chapter leader.

 392 + Returns (district_name, confidence) where confidence is high|medium|low|none.

 393 + Only called for territory-state entries with empty district. """

 394 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 395 + queries = [

 396 + f'" {name} " "computer science" teacher {state_name} ' ,

 397 + f'" {name} " CSTA {state_name} school district' ,

 398 + ]

 399 + all_snippets = []

 400 + for q in queries:

 401 + try :

 402 + orgs = serper_search(q, num= 5 )

 403 + for o in orgs[: 5 ]:

 404 + title = o.get( "title" , "" )

 405 + snippet = o.get( "snippet" , "" )

 406 + link = o.get( "link" , "" )

 407 + if snippet or title:

 408 + all_snippets.append( f"- [ {title[: 80 ]} ] {snippet[: 200 ]} (source: {link[: 80 ]} )" )

 409 + time.sleep( 0.4 )

 410 + except Exception as e:

 411 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Serper failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 412 +

 413 + if not all_snippets:

 414 + return "" , "none"

 415 +

 416 + snippets_text = "\n" .join(all_snippets[: 12 ])

 417 + prompt = f"""Based ONLY on these search results, what K-12 school district does {name} currently work at in {state_name} ?

 418 +

 419 + Rules:

 420 + - Return exactly: `district_name|confidence` where confidence is high, medium, low, or none.

 421 + - Return `|none` if no district is clearly named in the snippets.

 422 + - Do NOT guess. Do NOT fabricate. Prefer LinkedIn/school-directory hits.

 423 + - `district_name` must be a real K- 12 LEA or charter school.

 424 + - Example outputs:

 425 + Vallejo City Unified School District|high

 426 + Stoneham Public Schools|medium

 427 + |none

 428 +

 429 + Search results:

 430 + {snippets_text}

 431 +

 432 + Your answer (district_name|confidence): """

 433 +

 434 + try :

 435 + import anthropic

 436 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 60.0 )

 437 + resp = client.messages.create(

 438 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 439 + max_tokens= 150 ,

 440 + temperature= 0.0 ,

 441 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 442 + )

 443 + answer = resp.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 444 + # Strip code fences if any

 445 + answer = answer.replace( "`" , "" ).strip()

 446 + if "|" in answer:

 447 + district, confidence = answer.split( "|" , 1 )

 448 + return district.strip(), confidence.strip().lower()

 449 + except Exception as e:

 450 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Haiku failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 451 + return "" , "none"

 452 +

 453 +

 454 + def run_district_resolver ( entries: list [ dict ] ) -> tuple [ int , int ]:

 455 + """For each territory-state entry with empty district, try to resolve one.

 456 + Mutates entries in place. Returns (attempted, resolved). """

 457 + territory = TERRITORY_STATES_WITH_CA

 458 + attempted = 0

 459 + resolved = 0

 460 + for e in entries:

 461 + state = (e.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 462 + district = (e.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 463 + name = (e.get( "name" ) or "" ).strip()

 464 + if district:

 465 + continue

 466 + if state not in territory:

 467 + continue

 468 + if not name:

 469 + continue

 470 + attempted += 1

 471 + print ( f" resolving {name} ( {state} )..." )

 472 + d, conf = resolve_district(name, state)

 473 + if d and conf in ( "high" , "medium" ):

 474 + e[ "district" ] = d

 475 + e[ "district_resolver_confidence" ] = conf

 476 + resolved += 1

 477 + print ( f" ✓ {d} ( {conf} )" )

 478 + elif d:

 479 + print ( f" ~ {d} ( {conf} ) — low confidence, skipped" )

 480 + return attempted, resolved

 481 +

 ⏺ Two regressions surfaced:

 1. CA dropped from 12 → 0 entries. Because CA uses regional sub-chapter subdomains ( goldengate.csteachers.org , greaterlosangeles.csteachers.org , siliconvalley.csteachers.org ) that don't match my state-name

 regex. They went to national bucket, which returned 0.

 2. National bucket returned 0 items despite having 60+ URLs including the CSTA team/board/article pages. The strict national prompt over-filtered.

 But the resolver is working beautifully — resolved 12 of 16 attempts with real district names (Troy Public Schools, Minden Public Schools, Somerville Public Schools, etc.). Fixing the regressions now.

 national extraction fails because the 420K-char corpus of 84 URLs is too noisy.

 1. Remove broken URLs from explicit seeds

 2. Add known regional/community URLs for CA/PA/TX (goldengate, pittsburgh, etc.) + the CSTA community digest thread (baseline's source for 20+ entries)

 3. Whitelist-filter national URLs (only /team/ , /volunteer-spotlight , /meet-* , /csta-board-corner-* , /award-winners/* )

 4. Cap national corpus at 150K chars

 262 + def is_national_signal_url ( url: str ) -> bool :

 263 + """Return True if the URL should survive into the national bucket.

 264 + Filters out noise (PD articles, event calendars, jobs, schoolsofed, etc.). """

 265 + # Skip jobs subdomain, conference subdomain, landscape subdomain

 266 + lower = url.lower()

 267 + if any (x in lower for x in ( "jobs.csteachers.org" , "conference.csteachers.org" ,

 268 + "landscape.csteachers.org" , "my.csteachers.org" )):

 269 + return False

 270 + # Keep if any whitelist pattern matches

 271 + return any (p in url for p in NATIONAL_URL_WHITELIST_PATTERNS)

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── trim-claude-md-session-58 ──

 ⚠Large CLAUDE.md will impact performance (41.6k chars > 40.0k) · /memory to edit

 ⏺ Root cause isolated. Haiku is NOT silently dropping entries — it correctly extracts 14 entries (5 MI, 5 NE, 4 MA) from a focused 22K-char corpus containing only state chapter home pages. But in production,

 these same entries get lost when Haiku processes the full 610K-char corpus (national pages + all chapters + LinkedIn snippets).

 Saturation problem: Large mixed corpus drowns out small state chapter sections. Haiku prioritizes the louder national content and produces only ~33 entries total.

 Change 1 — Per-state Haiku extraction (fixes silent drop). Group fetched URLs by state prefix ( michigan.csteachers.org → MI bucket), plus a "national" bucket for csteachers.org/* URLs. Run Haiku once per

 bucket. Merge results. Cost: ~13 small calls instead of 1 big call, total ~$0.30 (still cheap).

 Expected yield: 33 → 60-80 total entries, 16 → 35-45 matchable (once we also pick up districts where Haiku can find them).

 Change 2 — District resolver pass (boosts matchable). For entries with district="" , run a Serper query like "{name}" computer science teacher {state_name} and feed top snippets to Haiku: "What K-12 district

 does this person work at?" Update only on confident answer. This handles the "chapter page lists name but not employer" problem that affects every state except CA.

 Expected yield from resolver: ~50% hit rate on the ~45 non-matchable entries → +20-22 additional districts. Final roster: 50-60 matchable (vs today's 16).

 Approve and I'll execute both changes in one commit, then rerun end-to-end and report final counts. Or we can do just Change 1 first if you want to see the cheaper win before adding the resolver.

 ❯ /effort high

 ⎿ Set effort level to high: Comprehensive implementation with extensive testing and documentation

 ❯ approved

 ⏺ Executing. Rewriting the fetcher with per-state bucketing + district resolver pass.

 ⏺ Write ( scripts/fetch_csta_roster.py )

 1 #!/usr/bin/env python3

 2 - """Build memory/csta_roster.json — one-shot CSTA chapter leader roster .

 2 + """Build memory/csta_roster.json — CSTA chapter leader roster with per-state

 3 + extraction + district resolver pass .

 4 - Two-phase pipeline:

 5 - Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on

 6 - csteachers.org subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 7 - Phase 2 (Extraction): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL

 8 - with a browser User-Agent, combine text with national seed pages and

 9 - LinkedIn snippets, feed to Claude Haiku for structured extraction.

 5 + Session 58 rewrite — fixes the saturation problem where a single Haiku call

 6 + on the full 610K-char corpus silently drops state chapter members in favor

 7 + of the louder national CSTA content. Splits extraction by state bucket.

 8 + Then resolves missing K- 12 employer districts via Serper + Haiku reverse

 9 + lookup for territory-state entries.

 11 - Why this shape: CSTA rosters are essentially static directory data. Daily

 12 - scanning is architecturally wrong (see project_f5_csta_scanner_low_yield.md

 13 - and BUG 2 plan). Fetch once, cache to JSON, enrich F2 (and future scanners)

 14 - inline via signal_processor.enrich_with_csta.

 11 + Pipeline:

 12 + Phase 1 (Discovery): Serper queries find CSTA chapter pages on csteachers.org

 13 + subdomains + LinkedIn snippets for each territory state.

 14 + Phase 2 (Fetch): httpx + BeautifulSoup fetch each csteachers.org URL with a

 15 + browser User-Agent. Bucket URLs by state subdomain prefix.

 16 + Phase 3 (Per-state extraction): Haiku extraction, one call per state bucket

 17 + plus one call for the national bucket. Per-state prompts inject the state

 18 + code so Haiku correctly tags employer state by default.

 19 + Phase 4 (District resolver): For territory-state entries still missing a

 20 + district, run Serper ` "{name}" "computer science" teacher {state_name}`

 21 + and feed snippets to Haiku for a reverse-lookup employer extraction.

 22 + Phase 5 (Dedup + write).

 24 Run: python3 scripts/fetch_csta_roster.py

 25 Output: memory/csta_roster.json

 18 - Cost: ~$0. 10 one-time (Serper + Haiku ) .

 26 + Cost: ~$ 0. 40 one-time (Serper + Haiku , up from ~$ 0 . 10 for the v1 single-call

 27 + version).

 28 Refresh: manually, quarterly or when CSTA announces new chapter boards.

 29 """

 196 + def url_to_state_code ( url: str ) -> str :

 197 + """Extract state code from URL subdomain pattern like 'https://michigan.csteachers.org/...'.

 199 + m = re.match( r"https?://([a-z]+)\.csteachers\.org" , url.lower())

 200 + if not m:

 201 + # Also check community subdomain patterns: community.csteachers.org/csta{state}/home

 202 + m2 = re.search( r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+?)silverstate" , url.lower())

 203 + if m2:

 204 + sub = m2.group( 1 )

 205 + return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "" )

 206 + m3 = re.search( r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+)/" , url.lower())

 207 + if m3:

 208 + sub = m3.group( 1 )

 209 + return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "" )

 215 + def bucket_urls_by_state ( urls: set [ str ] ) -> tuple [ dict [ str , list [ str ]], list [ str ]]:

 312 + def build_state_corpus ( state_code: str , urls: list [ str ], linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ] ) -> str :

 313 + """Build a focused corpus for one state bucket."""

 314 + blocks = []

 315 + for url in urls:

 316 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 317 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 318 + if not text:

 319 + continue

 320 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 321 +

 322 + # Add LinkedIn snippets for this state

 323 + state_snippets = [ls for ls in linkedin_snippets if ls.get( "state_hint" ) == state_code]

 324 + if state_snippets:

 325 + blocks.append( "\n===== LinkedIn snippets =====" )

 326 + for ls in state_snippets:

 327 + blocks.append(

 328 + f"\n-- [ {ls.get( 'state_hint' , '' )} ] {ls.get( 'title' , '' )} \n"

 329 + f"URL: {ls.get( 'url' , '' )} \n"

 330 + f" {ls.get( 'snippet' , '' )} \n"

 331 + )

 332 + return "" .join(blocks)

 333 +

 334 +

 335 + def build_national_corpus ( urls: list [ str ] ) -> str :

 336 + """Build the national bucket corpus."""

 337 + blocks = []

 338 + for url in urls:

 339 + print ( f" fetching {url} " )

 340 + text = fetch_page_text(url)

 341 + if not text:

 342 + continue

 343 + blocks.append( f"\n===== URL: {url} \n {text} \n" )

 344 + return "" .join(blocks)

 347 + def extract_per_state (

 348 + state_buckets: dict [ str , list [ str ]],

 349 + national_urls: list [ str ],

 350 + linkedin_snippets: list [ dict ],

 351 + ) -> list [ dict ]:

 352 + """Run Haiku once per state bucket + once for national. Merge results."""

 353 + all_items: list [ dict ] = []

 354 +

 355 + # Per-state calls

 356 + for state_code in sorted (state_buckets.keys()):

 357 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 358 + urls = state_buckets[state_code]

 359 + print ( f"\n === State bucket: {state_code} ( {state_name} ) — { len (urls)} URLs ===" )

 360 + corpus = build_state_corpus(state_code, urls, linkedin_snippets)

 361 + if not corpus.strip():

 362 + print ( f" (empty corpus, skipping)" )

 363 + continue

 364 + prompt = PER_STATE_PROMPT_TEMPLATE. format (

 365 + state_name=state_name,

 366 + state_code=state_code,

 367 + corpus=corpus[: 500000 ],

 368 + )

 369 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label=state_code)

 370 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for {state_code} " )

 371 + all_items.extend(items)

 372 +

 373 + # National bucket

 374 + print ( f"\n === National bucket — { len (national_urls)} URLs ===" )

 375 + national_corpus = build_national_corpus(national_urls)

 376 + if national_corpus.strip():

 377 + prompt = NATIONAL_PROMPT + national_corpus[: 500000 ]

 378 + items = haiku_extract(prompt, label= "national" )

 379 + print ( f" → Haiku returned { len (items)} items for national" )

 380 + all_items.extend(items)

 381 +

 382 + return all_items

 386 + # Phase 4 — District resolver (for entries with empty district)

 387 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 390 + def resolve_district ( name: str , state_code: str ) -> tuple [ str , str ]:

 391 + """Reverse-lookup employer district for a CSTA chapter leader.

 392 + Returns (district_name, confidence) where confidence is high|medium|low|none.

 393 + Only called for territory-state entries with empty district. """

 394 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_code, state_code).title()

 395 + queries = [

 396 + f'" {name} " "computer science" teacher {state_name} ' ,

 397 + f'" {name} " CSTA {state_name} school district' ,

 398 + ]

 399 + all_snippets = []

 400 + for q in queries:

 401 + try :

 402 + orgs = serper_search(q, num= 5 )

 403 + for o in orgs[: 5 ]:

 404 + title = o.get( "title" , "" )

 405 + snippet = o.get( "snippet" , "" )

 406 + link = o.get( "link" , "" )

 407 + if snippet or title:

 408 + all_snippets.append( f"- [ {title[: 80 ]} ] {snippet[: 200 ]} (source: {link[: 80 ]} )" )

 409 + time.sleep( 0.4 )

 410 + except Exception as e:

 411 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Serper failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 412 +

 413 + if not all_snippets:

 414 + return "" , "none"

 415 +

 416 + snippets_text = "\n" .join(all_snippets[: 12 ])

 417 + prompt = f"""Based ONLY on these search results, what K-12 school district does {name} currently work at in {state_name} ?

 418 +

 419 + Rules:

 420 + - Return exactly: `district_name|confidence` where confidence is high, medium, low, or none.

 421 + - Return `|none` if no district is clearly named in the snippets.

 422 + - Do NOT guess. Do NOT fabricate. Prefer LinkedIn/school-directory hits.

 423 + - `district_name` must be a real K- 12 LEA or charter school.

 424 + - Example outputs:

 425 + Vallejo City Unified School District|high

 426 + Stoneham Public Schools|medium

 427 + |none

 428 +

 429 + Search results:

 430 + {snippets_text}

 431 +

 432 + Your answer (district_name|confidence): """

 433 +

 434 + try :

 435 + import anthropic

 436 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 60.0 )

 437 + resp = client.messages.create(

 438 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 439 + max_tokens= 150 ,

 440 + temperature= 0.0 ,

 441 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 442 + )

 443 + answer = resp.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 444 + # Strip code fences if any

 445 + answer = answer.replace( "`" , "" ).strip()

 446 + if "|" in answer:

 447 + district, confidence = answer.split( "|" , 1 )

 448 + return district.strip(), confidence.strip().lower()

 449 + except Exception as e:

 450 + print ( f" [warn] resolver Haiku failed for {name} : {e} " , file=sys.stderr)

 451 + return "" , "none"

 452 +

 453 +

 454 + def run_district_resolver ( entries: list [ dict ] ) -> tuple [ int , int ]:

 455 + """For each territory-state entry with empty district, try to resolve one.

 456 + Mutates entries in place. Returns (attempted, resolved). """

 457 + territory = TERRITORY_STATES_WITH_CA

 458 + attempted = 0

 459 + resolved = 0

 460 + for e in entries:

 461 + state = (e.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 462 + district = (e.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 463 + name = (e.get( "name" ) or "" ).strip()

 464 + if district:

 465 + continue

 466 + if state not in territory:

 467 + continue

 468 + if not name:

 469 + continue

 470 + attempted += 1

 471 + print ( f" resolving {name} ( {state} )..." )

 472 + d, conf = resolve_district(name, state)

 473 + if d and conf in ( "high" , "medium" ):

 474 + e[ "district" ] = d

 475 + e[ "district_resolver_confidence" ] = conf

 476 + resolved += 1

 477 + print ( f" ✓ {d} ( {conf} )" )

 478 + elif d:

 479 + print ( f" ~ {d} ( {conf} ) — low confidence, skipped" )

 480 + return attempted, resolved

 481 +

 ⏺ Two regressions surfaced:

 1. CA dropped from 12 → 0 entries. Because CA uses regional sub-chapter subdomains ( goldengate.csteachers.org , greaterlosangeles.csteachers.org , siliconvalley.csteachers.org ) that don't match my state-name

 regex. They went to national bucket, which returned 0.

 2. National bucket returned 0 items despite having 60+ URLs including the CSTA team/board/article pages. The strict national prompt over-filtered.

 But the resolver is working beautifully — resolved 12 of 16 attempts with real district names (Troy Public Schools, Minden Public Schools, Somerville Public Schools, etc.). Fixing the regressions now.

 238 def url_to_state_code ( url: str ) -> str :

 239 """Extract state code from URL subdomain pattern like 'https://michigan.csteachers.org/...'.

 240 Returns '' if URL doesn 't match a state subdomain (e.g. bare csteachers.org)."""

 241 - m = re.match(r"https?://([a-z]+)\.csteachers\.org", url.lower())

 242 - if not m:

 243 - # Also check community subdomain patterns: community.csteachers.org/csta{state}/home

 244 - m2 = re.search(r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+?)silverstate", url.lower())

 245 - if m2:

 246 - sub = m2.group(1)

 247 - return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "")

 248 - m3 = re.search(r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+)/", url.lower())

 249 - if m3:

 250 - sub = m3.group(1)

 251 - return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "")

 241 + m = re.match( r"https?://([a-z - ]+)\.csteachers\.org" , url.lower())

 242 + if m:

 243 + sub = m.group( 1 )

 244 + # Try state-name match first

 245 + if sub in STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE:

 246 + return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE[sub]

 247 + # Then regional chapter match

 248 + if sub in REGIONAL_SUBDOMAIN_TO_STATE:

 335 - `state` MUST be a 2 -letter uppercase code. Do not return full state names.

 ⏺ Key finding: california.csteachers.org , pennsylvania.csteachers.org , texas.csteachers.org all DNS-fail — those state-level subdomains don't exist. Those states use regional chapter subdomains only. Plus the

 national extraction fails because the 420K-char corpus of 84 URLs is too noisy.

 1. Remove broken URLs from explicit seeds

 2. Add known regional/community URLs for CA/PA/TX (goldengate, pittsburgh, etc.) + the CSTA community digest thread (baseline's source for 20+ entries)

 3. Whitelist-filter national URLs (only /team/ , /volunteer-spotlight , /meet-* , /csta-board-corner-* , /award-winners/* )

 4. Cap national corpus at 150K chars

 262 + def is_national_signal_url ( url: str ) -> bool :

 263 + """Return True if the URL should survive into the national bucket.

 264 + Filters out noise (PD articles, event calendars, jobs, schoolsofed, etc.). """

 265 + # Skip jobs subdomain, conference subdomain, landscape subdomain

 266 + lower = url.lower()

 267 + if any (x in lower for x in ( "jobs.csteachers.org" , "conference.csteachers.org" ,

 268 + "landscape.csteachers.org" , "my.csteachers.org" )):

 269 + return False

 270 + # Keep if any whitelist pattern matches

 271 + return any (p in url for p in NATIONAL_URL_WHITELIST_PATTERNS)

 272 +

 ⏺ Two regressions surfaced:

 1. CA dropped from 12 → 0 entries. Because CA uses regional sub-chapter subdomains ( goldengate.csteachers.org , greaterlosangeles.csteachers.org , siliconvalley.csteachers.org ) that don't match my state-name

 regex. They went to national bucket, which returned 0.

 2. National bucket returned 0 items despite having 60+ URLs including the CSTA team/board/article pages. The strict national prompt over-filtered.

 But the resolver is working beautifully — resolved 12 of 16 attempts with real district names (Troy Public Schools, Minden Public Schools, Somerville Public Schools, etc.). Fixing the regressions now.

 317 - "chapter": "CSTA chapter name (e.g. 'CSTA Pittsburgh')",

 316 + "role" : "Chapter role or volunteer position. Use 'Volunteer' if no specific title." ,

 317 + "chapter" : "CSTA chapter name (e.g. 'CSTA Pittsburgh' , 'CSTA Dallas Fort Worth', 'CSTA Greater Boston' )" ,

 318 "state" : "2-letter state code of their K-12 SCHOOL EMPLOYER" ,

 319 "district" : "Exact K-12 school district name. Empty '' if not explicit." ,

 320 - "source_url": "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input."

 320 + "source_url" : "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input that mentions this person ."

 321 }

 323 - STRICT RULES:

 324 - - Only STATE or REGIONAL chapter officers/board members. SKIP the national CSTA Board of Directors.

 325 - - SKIP university professors and higher-ed.

 326 - - SKIP national CSTA HQ staff unless they ALSO hold a state/regional chapter role.

 323 + INCLUDE:

 324 + - State/regional chapter presidents, officers, board members, volunteer leads

 325 + - Chapter spotlight subjects named as chapter members/volunteers

 326 + - People described as working with or leading a specific CSTA state/regional chapter

 327 + - Team members of national CSTA who ALSO hold or previously held a state chapter role

 328 +

 329 + SKIP:

 330 + - Pure national CSTA Board of Directors entries (no state chapter tie)

 331 + - University professors and higher-ed (K- 12 only)

 332 + - Conference speakers with NO chapter affiliation mentioned

 333 +

 334 + RULES:

 335 - `state` MUST be a 2 -letter uppercase code. Do not return full state names.

 328 - - `district` must be a named K-12 LEA. Return "" if not explicit.

 336 + - `district` must be a named K- 12 LEA. Return "" if not explicit — the district resolver will pick it up later .

 337 - Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Empty array [] if nothing qualifies.

 ⏺ Key finding: california.csteachers.org , pennsylvania.csteachers.org , texas.csteachers.org all DNS-fail — those state-level subdomains don't exist. Those states use regional chapter subdomains only. Plus the

 national extraction fails because the 420K-char corpus of 84 URLs is too noisy.

 1. Remove broken URLs from explicit seeds

 2. Add known regional/community URLs for CA/PA/TX (goldengate, pittsburgh, etc.) + the CSTA community digest thread (baseline's source for 20+ entries)

 3. Whitelist-filter national URLs (only /team/ , /volunteer-spotlight , /meet-* , /csta-board-corner-* , /award-winners/* )

 4. Cap national corpus at 150K chars

 262 + def is_national_signal_url ( url: str ) -> bool :

 263 + """Return True if the URL should survive into the national bucket.

 264 + Filters out noise (PD articles, event calendars, jobs, schoolsofed, etc.). """

 265 + # Skip jobs subdomain, conference subdomain, landscape subdomain

 266 + lower = url.lower()

 267 + if any (x in lower for x in ( "jobs.csteachers.org" , "conference.csteachers.org" ,

 268 + "landscape.csteachers.org" , "my.csteachers.org" )):

 269 + return False

 270 + # Keep if any whitelist pattern matches

 271 + return any (p in url for p in NATIONAL_URL_WHITELIST_PATTERNS)

 272 +

 273 +

 274 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 326 def bucket_urls_by_state ( urls: set [ str ] ) -> tuple [ dict [ str , list [ str ]], list [ str ]]:

 327 - """Partition URLs into state buckets + a national bucket.

 328 - Returns (state_buckets, national_list). """

 327 + """Partition URLs into state buckets + a national bucket (whitelisted) .

 328 + Returns (state_buckets, national_list). Non-signal national URLs are

 329 + dropped entirely — the national bucket gets only team/volunteer/award/

 330 + digestviewer pages. """

 331 state_buckets: dict [ str , list [ str ]] = {}

 332 national: list [ str ] = []

 333 + dropped = 0

 334 for u in sorted (urls):

 335 code = url_to_state_code(u)

 336 if code:

 337 state_buckets.setdefault(code, []).append(u)

 335 - else:

 338 + continue

 339 + if is_national_signal_url(u):

 340 national.append(u)

 341 + else :

 342 + dropped += 1

 343 + if dropped:

 344 + print ( f" [note] Filtered {dropped} non-signal national URLs (PD articles, calendars, jobs)" )

 345 return state_buckets, national

 ⏺ Two regressions surfaced:

 1. CA dropped from 12 → 0 entries. Because CA uses regional sub-chapter subdomains ( goldengate.csteachers.org , greaterlosangeles.csteachers.org , siliconvalley.csteachers.org ) that don't match my state-name

 regex. They went to national bucket, which returned 0.

 2. National bucket returned 0 items despite having 60+ URLs including the CSTA team/board/article pages. The strict national prompt over-filtered.

 But the resolver is working beautifully — resolved 12 of 16 attempts with real district names (Troy Public Schools, Minden Public Schools, Somerville Public Schools, etc.). Fixing the regressions now.

 238 def url_to_state_code ( url: str ) -> str :

 239 """Extract state code from URL subdomain pattern like 'https://michigan.csteachers.org/...'.

 240 Returns '' if URL doesn 't match a state subdomain (e.g. bare csteachers.org)."""

 241 - m = re.match(r"https?://([a-z]+)\.csteachers\.org", url.lower())

 242 - if not m:

 243 - # Also check community subdomain patterns: community.csteachers.org/csta{state}/home

 244 - m2 = re.search(r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+?)silverstate", url.lower())

 245 - if m2:

 246 - sub = m2.group(1)

 247 - return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "")

 248 - m3 = re.search(r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z]+)/", url.lower())

 249 - if m3:

 250 - sub = m3.group(1)

 251 - return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "")

 241 + m = re.match( r"https?://([a-z - ]+)\.csteachers\.org" , url.lower())

 242 + if m:

 243 + sub = m.group( 1 )

 244 + # Try state-name match first

 245 + if sub in STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE:

 246 + return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE[sub]

 247 + # Then regional chapter match

 248 + if sub in REGIONAL_SUBDOMAIN_TO_STATE:

 249 + return REGIONAL_SUBDOMAIN_TO_STATE[sub]

 250 return ""

 253 - sub = m.group(1)

 254 - return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "")

 253 + if m2:

 254 + sub = m2.group( 1 )

 255 + return STATE_SUBDOMAIN_TO_CODE.get(sub, "" )

 256 + m3 = re.search( r"community\.csteachers\.org/csta([a-z-]+)/" , url.lower())

 + cles, event recaps, volunteer spotlights, award winners, and team bios are all valid sources if they mention someone's state chapter role.

 313 For each person, return JSON:

 314 {

 315 "name" : "Full name" ,

 316 - "role": "Chapter role",

 317 - "chapter": "CSTA chapter name (e.g. 'CSTA Pittsburgh')",

 316 + "role" : "Chapter role or volunteer position. Use 'Volunteer' if no specific title." ,

 318 "state" : "2-letter state code of their K-12 SCHOOL EMPLOYER" ,

 319 "district" : "Exact K-12 school district name. Empty '' if not explicit." ,

 320 - "source_url": "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input."

 320 + "source_url" : "Copy one of the '===== URL:' lines from the input that mentions this person ."

 321 }

 323 - STRICT RULES:

 324 - - Only STATE or REGIONAL chapter officers/board members. SKIP the national CSTA Board of Directors.

 325 - - SKIP university professors and higher-ed.

 326 - - SKIP national CSTA HQ staff unless they ALSO hold a state/regional chapter role.

 323 + INCLUDE:

 324 + - State/regional chapter presidents, officers, board members, volunteer leads

 325 + - Chapter spotlight subjects named as chapter members/volunteers

 326 + - People described as working with or leading a specific CSTA state/regional chapter

 327 + - Team members of national CSTA who ALSO hold or previously held a state chapter role

 328 +

 329 + SKIP:

 330 + - Pure national CSTA Board of Directors entries (no state chapter tie)

 331 + - University professors and higher-ed (K- 12 only)

 332 + - Conference speakers with NO chapter affiliation mentioned

 333 +

 334 + RULES:

 335 - `state` MUST be a 2 -letter uppercase code. Do not return full state names.

 328 - - `district` must be a named K-12 LEA. Return "" if not explicit.

 336 + - `district` must be a named K- 12 LEA. Return "" if not explicit — the district resolver will pick it up later .

 337 - Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Empty array [] if nothing qualifies.

 ⏺ Key finding: california.csteachers.org , pennsylvania.csteachers.org , texas.csteachers.org all DNS-fail — those state-level subdomains don't exist. Those states use regional chapter subdomains only. Plus the

 national extraction fails because the 420K-char corpus of 84 URLs is too noisy.

 1. Remove broken URLs from explicit seeds

 2. Add known regional/community URLs for CA/PA/TX (goldengate, pittsburgh, etc.) + the CSTA community digest thread (baseline's source for 20+ entries)

 3. Whitelist-filter national URLs (only /team/ , /volunteer-spotlight , /meet-* , /csta-board-corner-* , /award-winners/* )

 4. Cap national corpus at 150K chars
